1	\chapter{The Grassmannian over $\mathbb{Z}$: affine charts and gluing}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GrassmannianCells}
3	
4	\section{Set-up: charts indexed by $d$-subsets}
5	\label{sec:gr_setup}
6	
7	Fix integers \(r \ge d \ge 1\). For a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M\) over a ring
8	and a subset \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\), write \(M_I\)
9	for the \emph{\(I\)-th minor} of \(M\): the \(d \times d\) submatrix whose
10	columns are those indexed by \(I\). We construct the Grassmannian scheme
11	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\) by gluing one affine chart \(U^I\) for
12	each such \(I\). The construction is, conceptually, the quotient
13	\(\mathrm{GL}_{d,\mathbb{Z}} \backslash V\) of the scheme \(V\) of rank-\(d\)
14	matrices, but we never use the language of group-scheme actions: the charts and
15	their transition maps are elementary matrix algebra over \(\mathbb{Z}\). Taking
16	\(d = 1\) recovers \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,1) = \mathbb{P}^{r-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\).
17	
18	\begin{definition}
19	
20	\leanok
21	[Affine chart \(U^I\)]
22	  \label{def:gr_affine_chart}
23	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart}
24	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
25	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
26	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L807-L821).
27	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
28	  % "If $M$ is a $d\times r$-matrix, and
29	  %  $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with cardinality $\#(I)$ equal
30	  %  to $d$, the $I$ th minor $M_I$ of $M$ will
31	  %  mean the $d\times d$ minor of $M$ whose columns are indexed by
32	  %  $I$.
33	  %
34	  %  For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
35	  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
36	  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
37	  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$.
38	  %  Let $\Z[X^I]$ denote the polynomial ring in the variables
39	  %  $x^I_{p,q}$, and let $U^I = \spec \Z[X^I]$, which is non-canonically
40	  %  isomorphic to the affine space $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$."
41	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
42	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). Let \(X^I\) denote the
43	  \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\)
44	  identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\) and whose remaining \(d(r - d)\) entries are
45	  independent indeterminates \(x^I_{p,q}\) (\(1 \le p \le d\),
46	  \(q \in \{1,\dots,r\} \setminus I\)) over \(\mathbb{Z}\). Write
47	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) for the polynomial ring in these \(d(r-d)\) variables and
48	  set
49	  \[
50	    U^I \;:=\; \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I].
51	  \]
52	  The choice of an ordering of the free entries gives a (non-canonical)
53	  isomorphism \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\); in particular
54	  \(U^I\) is a smooth affine \(\mathbb{Z}\)-scheme of relative dimension
55	  \(d(r-d)\). Intuitively a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-point of \(U^I\) is a \(d \times r\)
56	  matrix whose \(I\)-columns form the identity, i.e.\ a rank-\(d\) quotient of
57	  \(\mathbb{Z}^{r}\) trivialised on the coordinates \(I\).
58	\end{definition}
59	
60	\section{Transition maps and the cocycle condition}
61	\label{sec:gr_transition}
62	
63	\subsection*{Mathlib inputs for the transition map}
64	
65	The construction of \(\theta_{I,J}\) is a Cramer-inverse computation over the
66	localised chart ring, followed by one application of the universal property of a
67	localisation. We record the four Mathlib facts it rests on as dependency
68	anchors.
69	
70	\begin{lemma}
71	[Localised element is a unit]
72	  \label{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
73	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit}
74	  \mathlibok
75	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
76	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(x \in R\), and \(S\) a localisation of \(R\)
77	  away from \(x\) (i.e.\ \(S = R[1/x]\)). Then the image of \(x\) under the
78	  structure map \(R \to S\) is a unit of \(S\).
79	\end{lemma}
80	
81	\begin{lemma}
82	[A square matrix is invertible iff its determinant is]
83	  \label{lem:mathlib_isUnit_iff_isUnit_det}
84	  \lean{Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det}
85	  \mathlibok
86	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
87	  For a square matrix \(A\) over a commutative ring, \(A\) is a unit of the
88	  matrix ring iff \(\det A\) is a unit of the scalar ring.
89	\end{lemma}
90	
91	\begin{lemma}
92	[Left Cramer inverse]
93	  \label{lem:mathlib_nonsing_inv_mul}
94	  \lean{Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul}
95	  \mathlibok
96	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
97	  For a square matrix \(A\) over a commutative ring with \(\det A\) a unit, the
98	  nonsingular inverse \(A^{-1}\) satisfies \(A^{-1} A = 1\).
99	\end{lemma}
100	
101	\begin{lemma}
102	[Right Cramer inverse]
103	  \label{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
104	  \lean{Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv}
105	  \mathlibok
106	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
107	  For a square matrix \(A\) over a commutative ring with \(\det A\) a unit, the
108	  nonsingular inverse \(A^{-1}\) satisfies \(A A^{-1} = 1\).
109	\end{lemma}
110	
111	\begin{lemma}
112	[Universal property of an away-localisation]
113	  \label{lem:mathlib_away_lift}
114	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.lift}
115	  \mathlibok
116	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
117	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(x \in R\), and \(S = R[1/x]\) a localisation
118	  of \(R\) away from \(x\). Given any ring homomorphism \(g : R \to P\) that
119	  sends \(x\) to a unit of \(P\), there is a unique ring homomorphism
120	  \(S \to P\) extending \(g\) along \(R \to S\).
121	\end{lemma}
122	
123	\subsection*{The transition map by matrix algebra}
124	
125	We now build \(\theta_{I,J}\) in seven steps over the localised chart ring
126	\(R^I_J := \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), splitting at the matrix-algebra seams. The
127	ambient chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) is the polynomial ring of
128	\cref{def:gr_affine_chart} in the \(d(r-d)\) free variables \(x^I_{p,q}\)
129	(\(p \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), \(q \notin I\)).
130	
131	\begin{definition}
132	[The universal matrix \(X^I\)]
133	  \label{def:gr_universal_matrix}
134	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix}
135	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart}
136	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
137	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
138	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L807-L821).
139	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
140	  % "For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
141	  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
142	  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
143	  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$."
144	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). With the chart ring
145	  %   `R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, define
146	  %   `universalMatrix d r I : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R^I` whose entry at
147	  %   `(p, q)` is: the identity entry `if (orderIso I) p = q then 1 else 0`
148	  %   when `q ∈ I` (using the order isomorphism `Fin d ≃o I` from `#I = d`),
149	  %   and the indeterminate `MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩)` when `q ∉ I`.
150	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
151	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). The \emph{universal
152	  matrix} \(X^I\) is the \(d \times r\) matrix over the chart ring
153	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) whose \(I\)-minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\) identity
154	  \(1_{d\times d}\) and whose remaining entries are the free indeterminates
155	  \(x^I_{p,q}\) (\(1 \le p \le d\), \(q \notin I\)). It is the universal point of
156	  the chart \(U^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}): a ring map
157	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to A\) is the same as a \(d \times r\) matrix over \(A\)
158	  with identity \(I\)-minor.
159	\end{definition}
160	
161	\begin{definition}
162	[The minor determinant \(P^I_J\)]
163	  \label{def:gr_minor_det}
164	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.minorDet}
165	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix}
166	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
167	  % affine patches" (read from
168	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
169	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
170	  % "For any $J\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(J) =d$, let
171	  %  $P^I_J = \det(X^I_J) \in \Z[X^I]$
172	  %  where $X^I_J$ is the $J$ th minor of $X^I$."
173	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). `minorDet d r I J : R^I` is the
174	  %   determinant of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose
175	  %   columns are indexed by `J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order
176	  %   isomorphism `Fin d ≃o J` coming from `#J = d`:
177	  %   `(universalMatrix d r I).submatrix id (orderIso J) |>.det`.
178	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
179	  For \(J \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(J) = d\), let \(X^I_J\) denote the
180	  \(J\)-th minor of the universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix})
181	  -- the \(d \times d\) submatrix whose columns are those indexed by \(J\). Set
182	  \[
183	    P^I_J \;:=\; \det\bigl(X^I_J\bigr) \;\in\; \mathbb{Z}[X^I].
184	  \]
185	  Then \(R^I_J := \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) (the localisation of the chart ring
186	  away from \(P^I_J\)) is the ring of the principal open subscheme
187	  \(U^I_J = \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J] \subseteq U^I\) on which \(P^I_J\) is
188	  invertible. One has \(P^I_I = \det(1_{d\times d}) = 1\), so \(R^I_I =
189	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) and \(U^I_I = U^I\).
190	\end{definition}
191	
192	\begin{definition}
193	[The localised \(J\)-minor \(X^I_J\) over \(R^I_J\)]
194	  \label{def:gr_universal_minor}
195	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinor}
196	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_minor_det}
197	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
198	  % affine patches" (read from
199	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
200	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
201	  % "Let $U^I_J = \spec \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$
202	  %  the open subscheme of $U^I$ where $P^I_J$ is invertible."
203	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
204	  %   `universalMinor d r I J : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J))`
205	  %   is the `J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` base-changed along the
206	  %   structure map `R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`:
207	  %   `((universalMatrix d r I).submatrix id (orderIso J)).map (algebraMap _ _)`.
208	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
209	  Write \(X^I_J \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(R^I_J)\) for the \(J\)-minor of
210	  the universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) with its entries
211	  pushed forward along the structure map \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to R^I_J\) into the
212	  localised chart ring \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\)
213	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}). By construction \(\det(X^I_J)\) is the image of
214	  \(P^I_J\) in \(R^I_J\).
215	\end{definition}
216	
217	\begin{lemma}
218	[The localised minor determinant is a unit]
219	  \label{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
220	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_det_universalMinor}
221	  \uses{def:gr_universal_minor, def:gr_minor_det, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
222	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
223	  % affine patches" (read from
224	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
225	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
226	  % "the open subscheme of $U^I$ where $P^I_J$ is invertible. This means
227	  %  the $d\times d$-matrix $X^I_J$ admits an inverse $(X^I_J)^{-1}$
228	  %  on $U^I_J$."
229	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
230	  %   `isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J).det`.
231	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
232	  The determinant of the localised \(J\)-minor \(X^I_J\)
233	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_minor}) is a unit of \(R^I_J\):
234	  \[
235	    \det\bigl(X^I_J\bigr) \in (R^I_J)^{\times}.
236	  \]
237	\end{lemma}
238	\begin{proof}
239	  \uses{def:gr_universal_minor, def:gr_minor_det, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
240	  By \cref{def:gr_universal_minor}, \(\det(X^I_J)\) is the image of \(P^I_J\)
241	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}) under the structure map
242	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to R^I_J\). Since \(R^I_J\) is the localisation away from
243	  \(P^I_J\), that image is a unit by
244	  \cref{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}.
245	\end{proof}

247	\begin{definition}
248	[The Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\)]
249	  \label{def:gr_universalMinorInv}
250	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinorInv}
251	  \uses{def:gr_universal_minor}
252	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
253	  % affine patches" (read from
254	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
255	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
256	  % "the $d\times d$-matrix $X^I_J$ admits an inverse $(X^I_J)^{-1}$
257	  %  on $U^I_J$."
258	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
259	  %   `universalMinorInv d r I J : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J))`
260	  %   `:= (universalMinor d r I J)⁻¹` (the Mathlib nonsingular inverse).
261	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
262	  Let \((X^I_J)^{-1} \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(R^I_J)\) be the
263	  nonsingular (Cramer) inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor \(X^I_J\)
264	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_minor}). Its entries lie in \(R^I_J\) by Cramer's rule.
265	\end{definition}
266	
267	\begin{lemma}
268	[The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse]
269	  \label{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}
270	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinorInv_mul_cancel}
271	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_minorDet_unit, lem:mathlib_nonsing_inv_mul, lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
272	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). A conjunction (or pair of
273	  %   lemmas):
274	  %   `universalMinorInv d r I J * universalMinor d r I J = 1` and
275	  %   `universalMinor d r I J * universalMinorInv d r I J = 1`.
276	  Because \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}),
277	  the Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}) is a
278	  genuine two-sided inverse:
279	  \[
280	    (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_J = 1_{d\times d}
281	    \qquad\text{and}\qquad
282	    X^I_J\, (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}.
283	  \]
284	\end{lemma}
285	\begin{proof}
286	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_minorDet_unit, lem:mathlib_nonsing_inv_mul, lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
287	  Immediate from \cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit} together with
288	  \cref{lem:mathlib_nonsing_inv_mul} (left identity) and
289	  \cref{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv} (right identity), both of which apply once
290	  \(\det(X^I_J)\) is known to be a unit.
291	\end{proof}

293	\begin{definition}
294	[The image matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)]
295	  \label{def:gr_image_matrix}
296	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix}
297	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_universal_matrix}
298	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
299	  % affine patches" (read from
300	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
301	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
302	  % "The images of the variables
303	  %  $x^J_{p,q}$ are given by the entries of the matrix formula
304	  %  $\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J) ^{-1}\,X^I$."
305	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
306	  %   `imageMatrix d r I J : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J))`
307	  %   `:= universalMinorInv d r I J * (universalMatrix d r I).map (algebraMap _ _)`.
308	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
309	  Define the \(d \times r\) matrix over \(R^I_J\)
310	  \[
311	    M \;:=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I \;\in\; \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times r}(R^I_J),
312	  \]
313	  the product of the Cramer inverse (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}) with the
314	  universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) base-changed to
315	  \(R^I_J\). Its entries are the prospective images of the indeterminates
316	  \(x^J_{p,q}\) under \(\theta_{I,J}\). Its \(J\)-minor is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_J =
317	  1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so \(M\) is a
318	  point of the chart \(U^J\); and its \(I\)-minor is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I =
319	  (X^I_J)^{-1}\).
320	\end{definition}

322	\begin{definition}
323	[The pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R^I_J\)]
324	  \label{def:gr_transition_pre}
325	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap}
326	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix}
327	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
328	  % affine patches" (read from
329	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
330	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
331	  % "a ring homomorphism
332	  %  $\theta_{I,J} : \Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \to \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$ is
333	  %  defined as follows. The images of the variables
334	  %  $x^J_{p,q}$ are given by the entries of the matrix formula
335	  %  $\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J) ^{-1}\,X^I$."
336	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). Via the universal property of the
337	  %   polynomial chart ring `R^J := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ`,
338	  %   `transitionPreMap d r I J : R^J →ₐ[ℤ] Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`
339	  %   `:= MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J e.1 e.2.1)`, i.e. the
340	  %   free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}` is sent to the `(p,q)`-entry of `imageMatrix`.
341	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
342	  Let \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R^I_J\) be the
343	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism out of the chart ring of \(J\) determined,
344	  via the universal property of the polynomial ring, by sending each free
345	  indeterminate \(x^J_{p,q}\) to the \((p,q)\)-entry of the image matrix \(M =
346	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_image_matrix}); equivalently
347	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) as a matrix identity (the
348	  identity \(J\)-block of \(X^J\) matching the identity \(J\)-minor of \(M\)).
349	\end{definition}

351	\begin{lemma}
352	[The pre-hom sends \(P^J_I\) to a unit]
353	  \label{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}
354	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
355	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
356	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
357	  % affine patches" (read from
358	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
359	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
360	  % "In particular, we have $\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1/P^I_J$,
361	  %  so the map extends to $\Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I]$."
362	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
363	  %   `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J :`
364	  %   `IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J (minorDet d r J I))`.
365	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
366	  The pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre})
367	  sends \(P^J_I\) (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}, the \(I\)-minor determinant of
368	  \(X^J\)) to a unit of \(R^I_J\). Indeed
369	  \[
370	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\bigr)
371	      = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1} = 1/P^I_J,
372	  \]
373	  which is a unit.
374	\end{lemma}
375	\begin{proof}
376	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
377	  By \cref{def:gr_transition_pre}, \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = M = (X^I_J)^{-1}
378	  X^I\), so \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) carries the \(I\)-minor \(X^J_I\) of \(X^J\) to
379	  the \(I\)-minor of \(M\), which is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) since
380	  the \(I\)-minor of \(X^I\) is the identity. Hence \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) =
381	  \det((X^I_J)^{-1}) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\). As \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit
382	  (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}), so is its inverse; the matrix-inverse
383	  determinant identity uses \cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}.
384	\end{proof}
385	
386	\begin{definition}
387	[Transition map \(\theta_{I,J}\)]
388	  \label{def:gr_transition}
389	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionMap}
390	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:mathlib_away_lift, def:gr_affine_chart}
391	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
392	  % affine patches" (read from
393	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
394	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
395	  % "For any $I$ and $J$, a ring homomorphism
396	  %  $\theta_{I,J} : \Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \to \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$ is
397	  %  defined as follows. ...
398	  %  In particular, we have $\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1/P^I_J$,
399	  %  so the map extends to $\Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I]$."
400	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). The away-localisation lift of the
401	  %   pre-hom along `P^J_I`:
402	  %   `transitionMap d r I J :`
403	  %   `Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I) →+* Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`
404	  %   `:= IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I)`
405	  %   `(isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J)` applied to
406	  %   `(transitionPreMap d r I J : R^J →+* R^I_J)`.
407	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
408	  Because the pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to
409	  R^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) sends \(P^J_I\) to a unit of \(R^I_J\)
410	  (\cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}), the universal property of the
411	  away-localisation (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) provides a unique ring
412	  homomorphism extending it along \(\mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to \mathbb{Z}[X^J, 1/P^J_I]\).
413	  This extension is the \emph{transition map}
414	  \[
415	    \theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \longrightarrow
416	    \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J], \qquad \theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I .
417	  \]
418	  Dually \(\theta_{I,J}\) is the comorphism of a morphism of schemes
419	  \(U^I_J \to U^J_I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}), which we also denote
420	  \(\theta_{I,J}\); it sends the universal matrix \(X^I\) to \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\).
421	\end{definition}
422	
423	\begin{lemma}
424	[\(\theta_{I,I}\) is the identity]
425	  \label{lem:gr_transition_self}
426	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionMap_self}
427	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_minor_det}
428	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
429	  % affine patches" (read from
430	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L838-L848).
431	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
432	  % "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$".
433	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
434	  %   `transitionMap_self d r I : transitionMap d r I I = RingHom.id _`
435	  %   (up to the identification `R^I_I = R^I` from `P^I_I = 1`).
436	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
437	  For every \(I\), \(\theta_{I,I} = \id_{U^I}\): since \(P^I_I = 1\)
438	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}) the minor \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\) is its own
439	  inverse, so \(\theta_{I,I}(X^I) = (X^I_I)^{-1} X^I = X^I\), i.e.\
440	  \(\theta_{I,I}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}) is the identity ring homomorphism
441	  of \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_I] = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\).
442	\end{lemma}
443	\begin{proof}
444	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_minor_det}
445	  When \(J = I\) one has \(P^I_I = \det(1_{d\times d}) = 1\)
446	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}), so \(R^I_I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\), the minor \(X^I_I\)
447	  is the identity, and its Cramer inverse is again the identity. Hence the image
448	  matrix is \(M = 1 \cdot X^I = X^I\), the pre-hom sends each \(x^I_{p,q}\) to
449	  itself, and its localisation lift (\cref{def:gr_transition}) is the identity.
450	\end{proof}
451	
452	\begin{lemma}
453	[Cocycle condition for the transition maps]
454	  \label{lem:gr_cocycle}
455	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.cocycleCondition}
456	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_transition_self}
457	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
458	  % affine patches" (read from
459	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L838-L848).
460	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
461	  % "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$, and
462	  %  we leave it to the reader to verify that
463	  %  for any three subsets $I$, $J$ and $K$ of $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of
464	  %  cardinality $d$, the co-cycle condition
465	  %  $\theta_{I,K}= \theta_{I,J}\theta_{J,K}$
466	  %  is satisfied."
467	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
468	  For any three subsets \(I, J, K \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) of cardinality
469	  \(d\), the transition maps of \cref{def:gr_transition} satisfy the cocycle
470	  condition. As morphisms of schemes, on the triple overlap inside \(U^I\),
471	  \[
472	    \theta_{I,K} \;=\; \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J},
473	  \]
474	  equivalently, as ring homomorphisms,
475	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\) (the two read the same
476	  identity through the order-reversal of \(\Spec\)). Together with
477	  \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\), this is exactly the compatibility datum required to
478	  glue the charts.
479	\end{lemma}
480	
481	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$, and
482	% we leave it to the reader to verify that
483	% for any three subsets $I$, $J$ and $K$ of $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of
484	% cardinality $d$, the co-cycle condition
485	% $\theta_{I,K}= \theta_{I,J}\theta_{J,K}$
486	% is satisfied."  (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
487	% L838-L848.)
488	\begin{proof}
489	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_transition_self}
490	  We verify the scheme-morphism form
491	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) by a direct matrix
492	  computation, valid over the open locus of \(U^I\) on which all the minors
493	  involved are invertible (namely \(P^I_J\) and \(P^I_K\), which suffices since
494	  the maps are determined by their restriction to a dense open and all rings are
495	  domains; the computation is an identity of matrices with entries in
496	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J, 1/P^I_K]\)).
497	
498	  Start from the matrix \(X^I\) (the universal point of \(U^I\)). By
499	  \cref{def:gr_transition},
500	  \[
501	    M^J \;:=\; \theta_{I,J}(X^I) \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
502	  \]
503	  a \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(J\)-minor is the identity, hence a point of
504	  \(U^J\). Its \(K\)-minor is
505	  \[
506	    M^J_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_K,
507	    \qquad\text{so}\qquad
508	    (M^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I_J,
509	  \]
510	  using \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\) for the invertible \(d \times d\) matrices
511	  \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\). Applying \(\theta_{J,K}\) to \(M^J\) gives
512	  \[
513	    \theta_{J,K}(M^J) \;=\; (M^J_K)^{-1} M^J
514	    \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I_J \,\cdot\, (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I
515	    \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I,
516	  \]
517	  the middle product \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) cancelling by
518	  associativity of matrix multiplication. The right-hand side is precisely
519	  \(\theta_{I,K}(X^I) = (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I\). Thus
520	  \(\theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) and \(\theta_{I,K}\) agree on the
521	  universal matrix \(X^I\), hence agree as morphisms \(U^I \supseteq U^I_J \cap
522	  U^I_K \to U^K\). The special case \(K = I\) (resp.\ \(J = I\)) together with
523	  \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\) shows each \(\theta_{I,J}\) is an isomorphism onto its
524	  image with inverse \(\theta_{J,I}\), since
525	  \(\theta_{J,I} \circ \theta_{I,J} = \theta_{I,I} = \id\).
526	\end{proof}
527	
528	\section{The glued Grassmannian scheme}
529	\label{sec:gr_glued}
530	
531	\begin{definition}
532	[The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
533	  \label{def:gr_glued_scheme}
534	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme}
535	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
536	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
537	  % affine patches" (read from
538	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L843-L850).
539	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
540	  % "Therefore the schemes
541	  %  $U^I$, as $I$ varies over all the
542	  %  ${r\choose d}$ different
543	  %  subsets of  $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of cardinality $d$,
544	  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$ to form a
545	  %  finite-type scheme $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$. As each $U^I$ is
546	  %  isomorphic to $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$, it follows that
547	  %  $\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is smooth of relative dimension $d(r-d)$."
548	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
549	  Gluing the affine charts \(\{U^I\}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}), one for each
550	  of the \(\binom{r}{d}\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) of cardinality
551	  \(d\), along the open subschemes \(U^I_J \subseteq U^I\) via the transition
552	  isomorphisms \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\)
553	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}) -- which satisfy the cocycle condition
554	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) and \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\)
555	  of \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} -- yields a scheme
556	  \[
557	    \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \longrightarrow \Spec \mathbb{Z}.
558	  \]
559	  It is of finite type over \(\mathbb{Z}\) (a finite gluing of finitely
560	  generated \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebras), and under the gluing each \(U^I\) is an
561	  open subscheme of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(U^I \cap U^J = U^I_J\).
562	
563	  Moreover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is \emph{smooth of relative
564	  dimension \(d(r-d)\)}. Smoothness and relative dimension are local on the
565	  source; the charts \(\{U^I\}\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and each
566	  \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) is
567	  affine space of dimension \(d(r-d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\), which is smooth over
568	  \(\mathbb{Z}\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\) (the polynomial ring
569	  \(\mathbb{Z}[x_1, \dots, x_{d(r-d)}]\) is a smooth \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra). As
570	  the property may be checked on an open cover of the source, the conclusion
571	  follows.
572	\end{definition}
573	
574	\section{Separatedness}
575	\label{sec:gr_separated}
576	
577	\begin{lemma}
578	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
579	  \label{lem:gr_separated}
580	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isSeparated}
581	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition}
582	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Separatedness" (read from
583	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860).
584	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
585	  % "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with $U^I\times U^J$
586	  %  can be seen to be the closed subscheme $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$
587	  %  defined by entries of the matrix formula $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$,
588	  %  and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
589	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
590	  The structure morphism \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
591	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is separated; equivalently, the diagonal
592	  \(\Delta : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \times_{\mathbb{Z}}
593	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is a closed immersion.
594	\end{lemma}
595	
596	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with
597	% $U^I\times U^J$ can be seen to be the closed subscheme
598	% $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$ defined by entries of the matrix formula
599	% $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$, and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
600	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860.)
601	\begin{proof}
602	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition}
603	  The products \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), as \(I, J\) range over the
604	  size-\(d\) subsets, form an affine open cover of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
605	  \times_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Separatedness may be checked on this
606	  cover: it suffices to show that for each pair \((I,J)\) the preimage
607	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J)\) maps by a closed immersion into
608	  \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), i.e.\ that the corresponding ring
609	  homomorphism is surjective.
610	
611	  By \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme} the diagonal preimage is the overlap
612	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J) = U^I \cap U^J = U^I_J = \Spec
613	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\). The restricted diagonal corresponds to the ring map
614	  \[
615	    \delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]
616	    \longrightarrow \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J],
617	    \qquad
618	    X^I \otimes 1 \mapsto X^I, \quad 1 \otimes X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
619	  \]
620	  the second component being the comorphism \(\theta_{I,J}\) of
621	  \cref{def:gr_transition} (a point of \(U^I_J\) and its image under
622	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) are the two coordinates of a point of the diagonal). We claim
623	  \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Its image contains all entries of \(X^I\)
624	  (from the first factor). From the second factor it contains the entries of
625	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\); taking the \(I\)-minor of this matrix, and using
626	  \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\), the image contains the entries of \((X^I_J)^{-1}\),
627	  and in particular
628	  \[
629	    \delta_{I,J}(1 \otimes P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = 1/P^I_J .
630	  \]
631	  Hence \(1/P^I_J\) lies in the image, so the image is all of
632	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) and \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Concretely the
633	  closed subscheme \(\Delta_{I,J} \subseteq U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\) is cut
634	  out by the entries of the matrix relation
635	  \[
636	    X^J_I \, X^I - X^J \;=\; 0,
637	  \]
638	  which on the diagonal holds because \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) forces
639	  \(X^J_I = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) and therefore \(X^J_I X^I =
640	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I = X^J\). As each restricted diagonal is a closed immersion,
641	  \(\Delta\) is a closed immersion and \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over
642	  \(\mathbb{Z}\).
643	\end{proof}
644	
645	\section{Properness}
646	\label{sec:gr_proper}
647	
648	\begin{lemma}
649	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is proper over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
650	  \label{lem:gr_proper}
651	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isProper}
652	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_separated}
653	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Properness" (read from
654	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L865-L891).
655	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
656	  % "We now show that $\pi : \grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is proper. It is enough to
657	  %  verify the valuative criterion of properness for discrete valuation rings.
658	  %  Let $\Rr$ be a dvr, $\K$ its quotient field, and let
659	  %  $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$ be a morphism. This is given by
660	  %  a ring homomorphism $f : \Z[X^I] \to \K$ for some $I$. Having fixed
661	  %  one such $I$, next choose $J$ such that $\nu(f(P^I_J))$ is minimum,
662	  %  where $\nu : \K \to \Z\bigcup \{ \infty\}$ denotes
663	  %  the discrete valuation. As $P^I_I = 1$, note that
664	  %  $\nu(f(P^I_J)) \le 0$, therefore
665	  %  $f(P^I_J) \ne 0$ in $\K$ and so the matrix $f(X^I_J)$
666	  %  lies in $GL_d(\K)$.
667	  %
668	  %  Now consider the homomorphism
669	  %  $g : \Z[X^J] \to \K$ defined by entries of the matrix formula
670	  %  $$g(X^J) = f((X^I_J)^{-1}\,X^I)$$
671	  %  Then $g$ defines the same morphism $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$,
672	  %  and moreover all $d\times d$ minors $X^J_K$ satisfy
673	  %  $\nu(g(P^J_K)) \ge 0$.
674	  %  As the minor $X^J_J$ is identity, it follows from the above that in fact
675	  %  $\nu(g(x^J_{p,q}))\ge 0$ for all entries of $X^J$.
676	  %  Therefore, the map $g : \Z[X^J] \to \K$
677	  %  factors uniquely via $\Rr \subset \K$. The resulting morphism of
678	  %  schemes $\spec \Rr \to U^J \hra \grass(r,d)$ prolongs
679	  %  $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$. We have already checked
680	  %  separatedness of $\grass(r,d)$, so now we see that
681	  %  $\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is proper."
682	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
683	  The structure morphism \(\pi : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
684	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is proper.
685	\end{lemma}
686	
687	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We now show that $\pi : \grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is
688	% proper. It is enough to verify the valuative criterion of properness for
689	% discrete valuation rings. Let $\Rr$ be a dvr, $\K$ its quotient field, and let
690	% $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$ be a morphism. This is given by a ring
691	% homomorphism $f : \Z[X^I] \to \K$ for some $I$. Having fixed one such $I$, next
692	% choose $J$ such that $\nu(f(P^I_J))$ is minimum, where
693	% $\nu : \K \to \Z\bigcup \{ \infty\}$ denotes the discrete valuation. As
694	% $P^I_I = 1$, note that $\nu(f(P^I_J)) \le 0$, therefore $f(P^I_J) \ne 0$ in $\K$
695	% and so the matrix $f(X^I_J)$ lies in $GL_d(\K)$. Now consider the homomorphism
696	% $g : \Z[X^J] \to \K$ defined by entries of the matrix formula
697	% $$g(X^J) = f((X^I_J)^{-1}\,X^I)$$ Then $g$ defines the same morphism
698	% $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$, and moreover all $d\times d$ minors
699	% $X^J_K$ satisfy $\nu(g(P^J_K)) \ge 0$. As the minor $X^J_J$ is identity, it
700	% follows from the above that in fact $\nu(g(x^J_{p,q}))\ge 0$ for all entries of
701	% $X^J$. Therefore, the map $g : \Z[X^J] \to \K$ factors uniquely via
702	% $\Rr \subset \K$. The resulting morphism of schemes
703	% $\spec \Rr \to U^J \hra \grass(r,d)$ prolongs
704	% $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$. We have already checked separatedness of
705	% $\grass(r,d)$, so now we see that $\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is proper."
706	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L865-L891.)
707	\begin{proof}
708	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_separated, def:gr_transition}
709	  Since \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is of finite type
710	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) and separated (\cref{lem:gr_separated}), and
711	  \(\mathbb{Z}\) is Noetherian, properness follows from the valuative criterion
712	  for discrete valuation rings: it suffices to show that every solid diagram
713	  \[
714	    \begin{array}{ccc}
715	      \Spec K & \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } & \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \\
716	      \downarrow & & \downarrow \pi \\
717	      \Spec R & \longrightarrow & \Spec \mathbb{Z}
718	    \end{array}
719	  \]
720	  with \(R\) a discrete valuation ring, \(K = \operatorname{Frac}(R)\) and
721	  valuation \(\nu : K \to \mathbb{Z} \cup \{\infty\}\), admits a unique diagonal
722	  filler \(\Spec R \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
723	
724	  \emph{Existence.} The morphism \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) lands
725	  in some chart \(U^I\) (\(\Spec K\) has one point), so it is given by a ring map
726	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\). Among the \(\binom{r}{d}\) minors choose \(J\)
727	  with \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) minimal. Because \(P^I_I = 1\) gives \(f(P^I_I) = 1\)
728	  with \(\nu = 0\), the minimum satisfies \(\nu(f(P^I_J)) \le 0\); in particular
729	  \(f(P^I_J) \ne 0\), so \(f(X^I_J) \in \mathrm{GL}_d(K)\) is invertible over
730	  \(K\). Define
731	  \[
732	    g : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \longrightarrow K, \qquad
733	    g(X^J) = f\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr) ;
734	  \]
735	  this is the composite of \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}) with
736	  \(f\), so \(g\) defines the same \(K\)-point \(\varphi\), now exhibited as
737	  landing in \(U^J\).
738	
739	  We show every entry \(g(x^J_{p,q})\) lies in \(R\), i.e.\ has \(\nu \ge 0\).
740	  First, for any size-\(d\) subset \(K' \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) the \(K'\)-minor
741	  of \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_{K'}\), so
742	  \[
743	    g(P^J_{K'}) = \det f\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_{K'}\bigr)
744	    = \frac{f(P^I_{K'})}{f(P^I_J)},
745	    \qquad
746	    \nu(g(P^J_{K'})) = \nu(f(P^I_{K'})) - \nu(f(P^I_J)) \ge 0
747	  \]
748	  by minimality of \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\). Now the \(J\)-minor \(X^J_J\) of \(X^J\) is
749	  the identity, so by cofactor expansion each individual entry
750	  \(x^J_{p,q}\) (with \(q \notin J\)) equals, up to sign, the minor
751	  \(P^J_{K'}\) for \(K' = (J \setminus \{j_p\}) \cup \{q\}\) -- the \(d \times d\)
752	  determinant obtained by replacing the \(p\)-th identity column of \(X^J_J\) by
753	  the column \(q\). Hence \(\nu(g(x^J_{p,q})) = \nu(g(P^J_{K'})) \ge 0\) for all
754	  entries, and the entries with \(q \in J\) are \(0\) or \(1\). Therefore
755	  \(g(\mathbb{Z}[X^J]) \subseteq R\), so \(g\) factors uniquely as
756	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R \hookrightarrow K\). The corresponding morphism
757	  \(\Spec R \to U^J \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) restricts to \(\varphi\)
758	  over \(\Spec K\) and lies over \(\Spec \mathbb{Z}\), providing the filler.
759	
760	  \emph{Uniqueness.} Any two fillers agree over the generic point \(\Spec K\)
761	  and, \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) being separated over \(\mathbb{Z}\)
762	  (\cref{lem:gr_separated}), two morphisms \(\Spec R \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\)
763	  agreeing on the schematically dense open \(\Spec K\) coincide. This verifies
764	  the valuative criterion, so \(\pi : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is
765	  proper.
766	\end{proof}
767	
768	\section{Out of scope}
769	\label{sec:gr_out_of_scope}
770	
771	This chapter constructs the Grassmannian \emph{scheme} \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over
772	the absolute base \(\mathbb{Z}\) and establishes its smoothness, separatedness
773	and properness. The following constructions are deferred to later chapters and
774	are not treated here:
775	\begin{itemize}
776	  \item \textbf{The tautological/universal quotient} \(u : \mathcal{O}^{\oplus
777	    r}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) and the determinant
778	    line bundle \(\det \mathcal{U}\) with its Pl\"ucker embedding.
779	  \item \textbf{The functor-of-points representability} of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\)
780	    -- that it represents \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d) =
781	    \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^r \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}} /
782	    \mathbb{Z} / \mathbb{Z}}\); this is blueprinted as
783	    \cref{thm:grassmannian_representable} in
784	    \cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}.
785	  \item \textbf{The relative version over a general base \(S\)} with a locally
786	    free sheaf \(V\) of rank \(r\): the scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V,d)\) obtained by
787	    base change and gluing over a trivialising cover of \(V\). The
788	    absolute-over-\(\mathbb{Z}\) construction of this chapter is the input to
789	    that base change.
790	\end{itemize}
791

# Analogy: transparent `R'`-module action for a base-change-square `ModuleCat` iso

## Mode
cross-domain-inspiration

## Slug
fbc-base-change-square-transparent-module

## Iteration
009

## Structural problem (abstracted)
We need an isomorphism of `ModuleCat R'` objects realising a ring base-change /
restriction-extension square (`(A⊗_R R')⊗_A M ≅ R'⊗_R M`), built so that the `R'`-action on
the carriers is *usable by typeclass resolution* — i.e. `smul_zero`/`smul_add` fire — rather
than being an opaque `_aux` `Module` def produced by Mathlib's `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars` /
`RestrictScalars` functor objects. The question is the canonical Mathlib idiom for stating and
consuming such a square iso transparently.

## Empirical root cause (confirmed this iter, not merely suspected)
At the `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (e := { g with map_smul' := ?_ })` site of
`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` (`FlatBaseChange.lean:909`), the `R'`-module instances on the
carriers are the opaque
`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv._aux_3 / ._aux_5 : Module ↑R' ↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars …).obj M)`.
The two `r' • 0 = 0` zero branches of the `TensorProduct.induction_on` proof of `map_smul'`
**cannot close by any of** `simp only [smul_zero, map_zero]` ("simp made no progress"),
`rw [smul_zero]` ("did not find pattern `?a • 0`"), or `erw [smul_zero]`
("**failed to synthesize `SMulZeroClass ↑R' ↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars …).obj M)`**").
The headline is the last: `SMulZeroClass R'` does not synthesize *from* the opaque `_aux_3`
`Module R'` instance, so the `smul_zero` lemma cannot even be stated against the goal, let
alone rewritten. By contrast, the `add`/`tmul` branches close by `erw [smul_add]` /
`erw [ExtendScalars.smul_tmul]` because those lemmas unify against `r' • (a+b)` / `r' • (a⊗s)`
subterms already present in the goal (whose `SMul` instance is fixed by the term), needing no
fresh class synthesis. The asymmetry is intrinsic: `smul_zero`'s `0` carries no `SMul` subterm
to pin the instance, so the elaborator falls back to synthesising `SMulZeroClass` and dies on
the opaque `_aux`. `letI`/`inferInstanceAs`/`.toDistribMulAction` re-supply a *fresh* `_aux`
that TC still ignores or that `whnf`-times-out. **The in-place `map_smul'`-over-functor-object
route is therefore structurally dead, not merely hard.**

Note also: the optimistic note at `FlatBaseChange.lean:851` ("`exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
(base_change_regroup_linearEquiv ↑M)` typechecks once the helper is in a separate compiled
module") is **stale/wrong**; the detailed note at `:911` is **correct**. Tested directly: the
imported helper has type `(A⊗[R]R')⊗[A]M ≃ₗ[R'] R'⊗[R]M` with the *canonical* `Module A (A⊗R')`
(`Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`) and `Module R M`, and `exact … .toModuleIso` fails with
"Application type mismatch … `(A⊗[R]R')⊗[A]M ≃ₗ[R'] …` but expected
`↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars includeLeftRingHom).obj M) ≃ₗ[R'] …`". The `⊗[A]` carriers differ
by the `Module A (A⊗R')` diamond (`includeLeftRingHom.toAlgebra` as used by `extendScalars`
vs `leftAlgebra` as used by `cancelBaseChange`); unifying them forces `whnf` of the opaque
object instance and does not go through.

## Failed approaches (from directive)
- Hand-built `g : LinearEquiv` over the bare functor-object carriers, `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso` →
  `map_smul'` zero branches blocked on opaque `Module ↑R'` (confirmed above).
- `erw [ExtendScalars.smul_tmul]` + `show` reductions → close `tmul`/`add`, leave the two `zero`
  branches with no tensor head to rewrite and no `SMulZeroClass` to synthesize.
- `letI`/`haveI`/`inferInstanceAs` re-supplying `SMulZeroClass`/`DistribMulAction` → fresh opaque
  `_aux`, ignored by TC; `.toDistribMulAction` projection `whnf`-times-out.

## Analogues found (ranked by porting cost, lowest first)

### Analogue: `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`)
- **Domain**: commutative algebra / ring base change (pushout of `CommRing`s).
- **Same structural problem there**: the *pushout-form* base-change cancellation. With
  `[Algebra.IsPushout R S A B]`,
  `cancelBaseChange R S A B M : B ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[S] S ⊗[R] M`.
  Instantiated at `R=R, S=R', A=A, B=A⊗[R]R', M=M` it is **exactly the regroup equiv**
  `(A⊗[R]R') ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[R'] R' ⊗[R] M`, and the `Algebra.IsPushout R R' A (A⊗[R]R')` instance
  it needs is supplied for free by `TensorProduct.isPushout'` (`Algebra.IsPushout R T S (R⊗S T)`
  with `S=A, T=R'`). Companions: `cancelBaseChange_tmul`, `cancelBaseChange_symm_tmul`,
  `toLinearEquiv_cancelBaseChangeAlg`.
- **Technique**: the equivalence is **natively `≃ₗ[S] = ≃ₗ[R']`** — Mathlib already discharges
  `R'`-linearity inside the bundled `LinearEquiv`. There is **no hand-written `map_smul'`**, hence
  **no `TensorProduct.induction_on`, hence no `r' • 0 = 0` zero branch at all**. This is the
  decisive structural improvement over the project's current core
  `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R A A M R'`, which is only `≃ₗ[A]` (its `B`
  slot is taken as `A`), forcing the project to *re-bundle* `R'`-linearity by hand via
  `rightAlgebra` + `comm` + a manual `map_smul'` — the exact source of the dead zero branches.
- **Mapping to project**: replace the `RegroupHelper.lean` core
  `base_change_regroup_linearEquiv` (`comm ≪≫ ATM.cancelBaseChange ≪≫ comm` + hand `map_smul'`)
  by `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange ↑R ↑R' ↑A (↑A ⊗[↑R] ↑R') ↑M` (or keep the helper but
  swap its body). Then `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` is `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso` of it, modulo
  the **one** residual obstruction shared by every approach: the `Module A (A⊗R')` diamond between
  `cancelBaseChange`'s canonical carrier and `extendScalars`'s functor-object carrier.
- **Porting cost**: low-to-medium. The R'-linearity problem evaporates; the only remaining work is
  the A-diamond carrier bridge (see "How to resolve the residual diamond" below), which is an
  *object-level* `≃ₗ`/`eqToIso` reconciliation that never writes an element-level `r' • 0`.
- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.

### Analogue: `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` / `restrictScalarsId` / `extendScalarsComp` / `extendScalarsId` / `restrictScalarsCongr` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings`)
- **Domain**: category theory of change-of-rings functors.
- **Same structural problem there**: building a change-of-rings *tower* iso transparently. The key
  fact is that `restrictScalarsComp'App_hom_apply` / `_inv_apply` are **`= x` (the identity on
  the carrier set)** — the iso relabels the `Module` structure while fixing the underlying
  element. Hence an iso assembled from these never produces an element-level `r' • 0` goal: all
  module-action coherence is internal to the bundled `Iso`.
- **Technique**: assemble the comparison as a composite of `Iso`s in `ModuleCat R'`
  (`restrictScalarsComp'App` ×n, `extendScalarsId`/`Comp`, `restrictScalarsCongr` from a ring
  equation, `eqToIso`, `Functor.mapIso` of a smaller iso) — **never** `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso`
  of a hand `AddEquiv` + `map_smul'`. The project's own `gammaPushforwardIso`
  (`FlatBaseChange.lean:288`) is the existence proof that this route closes **axiom-clean** for a
---DAG---

import Mathlib
set_option autoImplicit false
open AlgebraicGeometry
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
  fun p q =>
    if h : q ∈ I then
      (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)

noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det

noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map
    (algebraMap _ _)

theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
  rw [universalMinor, ← RingHom.map_det]
  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)

noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹

theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩

noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)

noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ] Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

set_option autoImplicit false
open AlgebraicGeometry
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
  fun p q =>
    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)

noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det

noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map
    (algebraMap _ _)

theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
  rw [h]
  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _

#print axioms isUnit_det_universalMinor
end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Grassmannian transition maps

The transition map `θ_{I,J}` of `def:gr_transition` is a Cramer-inverse computation
over the localised chart ring `R^I_J := ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`, followed by the universal
property of the away-localisation. We build it bottom-up over the chart ring
`R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, all elementary matrix algebra
over `ℤ`. The `d`-subset combinatorics are handled by the order isomorphism
`Finset.orderIsoOfFin : I.card = d → (Fin d ≃o ↥I)`.

Blueprint references: `def:gr_universal_matrix`, `def:gr_minor_det`,
`def:gr_universal_minor`, `lem:gr_minorDet_unit`, `def:gr_universalMinorInv`,
`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`, `def:gr_image_matrix`, `def:gr_transition_pre`
(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/

/-- The **universal matrix** `X^I` (`def:gr_universal_matrix`): the `d × r` matrix
over the chart ring `R^I = MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` whose `I`-minor is the
`d × d` identity (read through the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥I`) and whose remaining
`d(r-d)` entries are the free indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` (`q ∉ I`). Project-local:
the universal point of the affine chart `affineChart d r I`. -/
noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
  fun p q =>
    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)

/-- The **minor determinant** `P^I_J = det(X^I_J)` (`def:gr_minor_det`): the determinant
of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose columns are those indexed by
`J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥J`. Project-local: defines the
principal open `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`. -/
noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det

/-- The **localised `J`-minor** `X^I_J` over `R^I_J` (`def:gr_universal_minor`): the
`J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` with entries pushed forward along the structure map
`R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)

/-- The localised minor determinant is a unit (`lem:gr_minorDet_unit`): `det(X^I_J)` is the
image of `P^I_J` under the away-localisation structure map, hence a unit. Project-local. -/
theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
  rw [h]
  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _

/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
rule. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹

/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
Project-local. -/
theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩

/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)

/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)

end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

import Mathlib
set_option autoImplicit false
open AlgebraicGeometry
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
  fun p q =>
    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)

theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  ext p p'
  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
  refine if_congr ?_ rfl rfl
  rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]

#print axioms universalMatrix_submatrix_self
end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

import Mathlib

open AlgebraicGeometry
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=

    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)

theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  ext p p'
  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
  by_cases h : p = p'
  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]

#print axioms universalMatrix_submatrix_self
end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

import Mathlib
set_option autoImplicit false
open AlgebraicGeometry
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
  fun p q => if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) := (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
  rw [h]; exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) := (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)

theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  ext p p'
  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
  by_cases h : p = p'
  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]

private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]

theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  rw [imageMatrix, mul_submatrix_col, Matrix.submatrix_map]
  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1

theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
  rw [imageMatrix, mul_submatrix_col, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]

#print axioms imageMatrix_submatrix_self
#print axioms imageMatrix_submatrix_I
end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :

  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]

example {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
  rw [mul_submatrix_col]

    ((A * B).submatrix id g).det = 0 := by
  rw [mul_submatrix_col]
  sorry

end Test

    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) := (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
  rw [h]; exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) := (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  ext p p'
  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
  by_cases h : p = p'
  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]

theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ := by
    ext i j
    simp only [imageMatrix, universalMinor, Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, Matrix.mul_apply,
      Matrix.map_apply]
  rw [h1]; exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1

theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
        ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _) := by
    ext i j
    simp only [imageMatrix, Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.map_apply]
  rw [h1, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]

#print axioms imageMatrix_submatrix_self
#print axioms imageMatrix_submatrix_I
end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

import Mathlib
set_option autoImplicit false
set_option linter.style.longLine false
open AlgebraicGeometry
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
  fun p q => if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) := (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
  rw [h]; exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) := (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  ext p p'
  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
  by_cases h : p = p'
  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]

private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]

theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1

theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]

#print axioms imageMatrix_submatrix_self
#print axioms imageMatrix_submatrix_I
end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  ext p p'
  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
  by_cases h : p = p'
  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]

/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]

/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
Project-local. -/
theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1

/-- The `I`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the Cramer inverse:
`M_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]

/-- The pre-localisation hom realises the matrix formula `θ̃_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`:
applying `θ̃_{I,J}` entrywise to the universal matrix `X^J` yields the image matrix `M`
(`def:gr_transition_pre`, `def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
theorem universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
      = imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ := by
  ext p q
  simp only [Matrix.map_apply, universalMatrix]
  by_cases hq : q ∈ J
  · rw [dif_pos hq]
    set k := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ).symm ⟨q, hq⟩ with hk
    have hqk : (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) = q := by simp [hk]
    have himg : imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ p q = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _) p k := by
      have e := congrFun (congrFun (imageMatrix_submatrix_self d r I J hI hJ) p) k
      rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq] at e
      rw [← hqk]; exact e
    rw [himg, Matrix.one_apply, apply_ite (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ), map_one, map_zero]
    have hcond : ((J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p : Fin r) = q) ↔ (p = k) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [← hqk]
      rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
    by_cases hpk : p = k
    · rw [if_pos (hcond.mpr hpk), if_pos hpk]
    · rw [if_neg (hcond.not.mpr hpk), if_neg hpk]
  · rw [dif_neg hq, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X]

/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to a unit (`lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`):
`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) = det((X^I_J)⁻¹) = det(X^I_J)⁻¹ = 1/P^I_J`, a unit of `R^I_J`. This is the
hypothesis that lets `θ̃_{I,J}` extend along the away-localisation `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
Project-local. -/
theorem isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)) := by
  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
  have hmul : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det * (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = 1 := by
    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
  exact isUnit_of_mul_eq_one _ _ hmul

end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
