413	\begin{theorem}[Hilbert polynomial from the section module]
414	  \label{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
415	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomialOfSectionModule}
416	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
417	    lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
418	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
419	  % Polynomials" (read from
420	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L478).
421	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
422	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
423	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
424	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
425	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$. Then for each $s\in S$,
426	  %  we get a polynomial ${\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]$
427	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
428	  %  the restriction $\F_s = F|_{X_s}$ of $\F$ to the fiber $X_s$ over $s$,
429	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle $L_s = L|_{X_s}$."
430	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
431	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
432	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
433	  Then there is a unique polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
434	  such that for all \(m \gg 0\)
435	  \[
436	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
437	    \;=\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr).
438	  \]
439	  This \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is the Hilbert polynomial of
440	  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}.
441	\end{theorem}
442	
443	\begin{proof}
444	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
445	    lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
446	  By \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} the section graded module
447	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded module over the
448	  Noetherian graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\), with finite-dimensional components. By
449	  \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational} there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and
450	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N\) with
451	  \(\dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
452	  = [X^m]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\) for \(m > N\). Apply
453	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib} over the characteristic-zero field
454	  \(F = \mathbb{Q}\) to this pair \((p, d)\): it yields a unique polynomial
455	  \(h \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) whose values \(h(n)\) eventually equal
456	  \([X^n]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\). Setting
457	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} := h\) (with the variable renamed \(\lambda\)) gives a
458	  polynomial with \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = \dim_{\kappa(s)}
459	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) for \(m \gg 0\). Uniqueness
460	  is the \(\exists!\) clause of \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}: any two
461	  polynomials agreeing with the Hilbert function for \(m \gg 0\) agree at
462	  infinitely many points, hence are equal.
463	\end{proof}

465	\section{Support and freeness predicates}
466	\label{sec:quot_predicates}
467	
468	The Quot functor (\cref{def:quot_functor}) and the Grassmannian
469	(\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme}) classify quotient sheaves subject to two
470	geometric conditions: a coherent quotient must have \emph{schematic support
471	proper over the base}, and the Grassmannian's quotients must be \emph{locally
472	free of a fixed rank}. This section introduces both predicates.
473	
474	\begin{definition}
475	[Annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules]
476	  \label{def:modules_annihilator}
477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator}
478	  \uses{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map, lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
479	  % NOTE: basic-open coherence depends on lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen
480	  % (section restriction as a localization for quasi-coherent sheaves on a general scheme),
481	  % which is not yet formally closed.
482	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
483	  % Polynomials" (read from
484	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
485	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
486	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
487	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
488	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
489	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
490	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); the construction is a
491	  project-built primitive.}
492	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of
493	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is \emph{quasi-coherent and of finite type}
494	  (over a locally noetherian base, so that on each affine open \(U\) the section
495	  module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is a finitely generated \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module).
496	  The \emph{annihilator ideal sheaf} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the ideal sheaf
497	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) defined on affine opens: for each affine open
498	  \(U \subseteq X\),
499	  \[
500	    \mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)
501	    \;=\; \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(U)\bigr)
502	    \;\subseteq\; \mathcal{O}_X(U),
503	  \]
504	  the annihilator of the \(\mathcal{O}_X(U)\)-module \(\mathcal{F}(U)\). These
505	  local data patch to a coherent ideal sheaf on \(X\) whose vanishing locus is
506	  the set-theoretic support of \(\mathcal{F}\).
507	
508	  \emph{Well-definedness on basic opens.} The coherence condition for an ideal
509	  sheaf requires that, for an affine open \(U\) and \(f \in \mathcal{O}_X(U)\),
510	  the section \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})(U)\) restricts correctly to the basic
511	  open \(D(f) \subseteq U\), i.e. that
512	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(D(f))}(\mathcal{F}(D(f)))\) is the extension of
513	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U))\) along the localization map
514	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(U) \to \mathcal{O}_X(D(f))\). This is exactly where the two
515	  hypotheses enter. The bridge
516	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen} supplies, for a quasi-coherent
517	  finite-type \(\mathcal{F}\), that \(\mathcal{O}_X(D(f)) = (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\)
518	  and that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{F}(D(f))\) exhibits
519	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(f))\) as the localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
520	  \mathcal{F}(U)\); the algebra engine
521	  \cref{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map} then yields, using that
522	  \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) is finitely generated, the required identity
523	  \(\mathrm{Ann}_{(\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f}\bigl((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}
524	  \mathcal{F}(U)\bigr) = \mathrm{Ann}_{\mathcal{O}_X(U)}(\mathcal{F}(U)) \cdot
525	  (\mathcal{O}_X(U))_f\). Thus the local annihilators are compatible with basic-open
526	  restriction, which is the patching datum that makes \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\)
527	  a well-defined ideal sheaf. The finite-type hypothesis is genuinely required:
528	  without finite generation of \(\mathcal{F}(U)\) the annihilator need not commute
529	  with localization, and the local data fail to patch.
530	
531	  \emph{Note.} This is a project-built primitive; Mathlib does not carry an
532	  annihilator for sheaves of modules on a scheme.
533	\end{definition}
534	
535	\begin{lemma}\leanok
536	[Annihilator of a finitely generated module commutes with
537	  localization]
538	  \label{lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map}
539	  \lean{Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map}
540	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, let \(S \subseteq R\) be a multiplicative
541	  submonoid, and let \(R_S = S^{-1}R\) be the localization of \(R\) at \(S\). Let
542	  \(M\) be a \emph{finitely generated} \(R\)-module and let \(f : M \to M_S\) be
543	  an \(R\)-linear map exhibiting \(M_S\) as the localized module \(S^{-1}M\)
544	  (i.e.\ \(f\) is a localization of \(M\) at \(S\)). Then the annihilator of
545	  \(M_S\) over \(R_S\) is the extension of the annihilator of \(M\) along the
546	  structure map \(R \to R_S\):
547	  \[
548	    \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)
549	    \;=\; (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S
550	    \;=\; \mathrm{map}\bigl(R \to R_S\bigr)\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}_R M\bigr).
551	  \]
552	  Equivalently, for finitely generated \(M\) the annihilator commutes with
553	  localization: \(\mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M) = S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M)\).
554	
555	  \emph{The finite-generation hypothesis is essential.} For a module that is not
556	  finitely generated the inclusion \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}(M) \subseteq
557	  \mathrm{Ann}(S^{-1}M)\) can be strict, so the identity fails. This is the
558	  standard commutative-algebra fact that the annihilator of a finite module
559	  localizes; it is recorded here as the load-bearing algebra engine for the
560	  basic-open coherence of \cref{def:modules_annihilator}.
561	\end{lemma}
562	
563	\begin{proof}
564	  \(\supseteq\): if \(r \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\), then \(r\) kills every element of
565	  \(M\), hence its image \(r/1 \in R_S\) kills every element of \(M_S\) (each such
566	  element is \(m/s = (1/s)\cdot f(m)\) for some \(m \in M\), \(s \in S\), and
567	  \((r/1)\cdot(m/s) = (1/s)\cdot f(r\,m) = 0\)). Thus
568	  \((\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S \subseteq \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\).
569	
570	  \(\subseteq\): write \(M = \langle m_1, \dots, m_n\rangle\) using the
571	  finite-generation hypothesis, and let \(y \in \mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S)\). Write
572	  \(y = a/s\) with \(a \in R\), \(s \in S\). For each generator \(m_i\) the element
573	  \(y\cdot f(m_i) = (a/s)\cdot f(m_i) = 0\) in \(M_S\); by the definition of the
574	  localized module this means there is \(u_i \in S\) with \(u_i\,a\,m_i = 0\) in
575	  \(M\). Put \(u = \prod_{i=1}^n u_i \in S\). Then \(u\,a\) kills every generator
576	  \(m_i\), hence kills all of \(M\), so \(u\,a \in \mathrm{Ann}_R M\). Finally
577	  \(y = a/s = (u\,a)/(u\,s)\) lies in \(S^{-1}\,\mathrm{Ann}_R M =
578	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\). Therefore \(\mathrm{Ann}_{R_S}(M_S) \subseteq
579	  (\mathrm{Ann}_R M)\cdot R_S\), and combined with the reverse inclusion the two
580	  ideals coincide. The use of a single common multiplier \(u = \prod_i u_i\) is
581	  exactly the step that requires finitely many generators.
582	\end{proof}
583	
584	\begin{lemma}[Sections on a basic open localize the affine ring]
585	  \label{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
586	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen}
587	  \mathlibok
588	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
589	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}).}
590	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(U \subseteq X\) an affine open, and
591	  \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then the restriction map
592	  \(\Gamma(X, U) \to \Gamma\bigl(X,\, D(f)\bigr)\) on the basic open
593	  \(D(f) = X.\mathrm{basicOpen}\,f\) exhibits \(\Gamma(X, D(f))\) as the
594	  localization of \(\Gamma(X, U)\) away from \(f\); that is, it is
595	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalization.Away}\,f\), so
596	  \(\Gamma(X, D(f)) = (\Gamma(X, U))_f\) as a \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-algebra.
597	\end{lemma}
598	
599	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent sections on a basic open are the localized module]
600	  \label{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}
601	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen}
602	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
603	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes" (chapter tag 020J), \S 7
604	  % "Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines", Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves},
605	  % item (4) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691-L702).
606	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
607	  % "Let $R$ be a ring. Let $M$ be an $R$-module. Let $\widetilde M$
608	  %  be the sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$-modules
609	  %  associated to $M$.
610	  %  [...]
611	  %  \item For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
612	  %  as an $R_f$-module."
613	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). For a quasi-coherent sheaf of
614	  %   modules `F : X.Modules` (`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent F`), an affine open
615	  %   `U : X.Opens` (`IsAffineOpen U`), and `f : Γ(X, U)` (the ring of sections,
616	  %   `X.presheaf.obj (op U)`), the section-restriction along the basic-open
617	  %   inclusion `D(f) = X.basicOpen f ⊆ U` exhibits `Γ(F, D(f))` as the
618	  %   localization of the `Γ(X, U)`-module `Γ(F, U)` at `Submonoid.powers f`.
619	  %   Write `ρ : Γ(F, U) →ₗ[Γ(X, U)] Γ(F, D(f))` for that restriction map (the
620	  %   additive `F.presheaf.map (homOfLE hD).op` made `Γ(X,U)`-linear by restriction
621	  %   of scalars along the ring map `Γ(X, U) → Γ(X, D(f))`). Then:
622	  %     {X : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.Modules) [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent F]
623	  %       {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U)) :
624	  %       IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) ρ
625	  %   The companion ring statement `Γ(X, D(f)) = (Γ(X, U))_f`
626	  %   (`IsLocalization.Away f (Γ(X, D(f)))`) is lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib;
627	  %   the scalar tower `Γ(X, U) → Γ(X, D(f)) → Γ(F, D(f))` and the exact
628	  %   `Hom`/`presheaf.map` spelling of `ρ` are pinned by the scaffold pass.
629	  %   NOTE: only quasi-coherence is needed for this localization bridge — the
630	  %   finite-type hypothesis enters later (in def:modules_annihilator, via
631	  %   lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map), not here.
632	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", \S 7, Lemma on the sheaves
633	  \(\widetilde M\) on \(\Spec(R)\), item (4).}
634	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a \emph{quasi-coherent} sheaf
635	  of modules on \(X\). Let \(U \subseteq X\) be an
636	  affine open and \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then:
637	  \begin{enumerate}
638	    \item \(\Gamma\bigl(X, D(f)\bigr)\) is the localization \((\Gamma(X,U))_f\)
639	      of \(\Gamma(X,U)\) away from \(f\)
640	      (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}); and
641	    \item the section-restriction map
642	      \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}\bigl(D(f)\bigr)\) exhibits
643	      \(\mathcal{M}(D(f))\) as the localized module
644	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\mathcal{M}(U)\); that is, it is a localization
645	      of the \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-module \(\mathcal{M}(U)\) at the submonoid
646	      \(\mathrm{powers}\,f = \{1, f, f^2, \dots\}\).
647	  \end{enumerate}
648	\end{lemma}
649	
650	\begin{proof}
651	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
652	  Part (1) is \cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, the Mathlib statement
653	  that on an affine open \(U\) the sections over the basic open \(D(f)\) form the
654	  localization of \(\Gamma(X,U)\) away from \(f\).
655	
656	  For part (2), identify the affine open \(U\) with \(\Spec \Gamma(X, U)\). Over
657	  this affine, a quasi-coherent sheaf is \(\widetilde{N}\) for the
658	  \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-module \(N = \mathcal{M}(U)\), the sheaf associated to
659	  \(N\) (no finiteness of \(N\) is required for this step). The Spec-local
660	  computation of the sections of \(\widetilde{N}\) on a
661	  basic open gives \(\Gamma\bigl(D(f), \widetilde{N}\bigr) = N_f\) as an
662	  \((\Gamma(X,U))_f\)-module, and the restriction map
663	  \(\Gamma(\Spec \Gamma(X,U), \widetilde{N}) = N \to N_f\) is precisely the
664	  localization homomorphism \(N \to (\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1} N\). Transporting
665	  this identification across the affine isomorphism
666	  \(U \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, U)\) -- under which \(\mathcal{M}|_U\) corresponds to
667	  \(\widetilde{N}\) by quasi-coherence and the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq U\)
668	  corresponds to the standard open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec\Gamma(X,U)\) -- shows
669	  that the restriction \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}(D(f))\) is the
670	  localization of \(N = \mathcal{M}(U)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\), i.e.\ it is a
671	  localized module at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\). The substantive content is this
672	  transport of the Spec-local localization fact for the sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\)
673	  to a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{M}\) via the affine identification.
674	\end{proof}

676	\begin{definition}
677	[Schematic support of a sheaf of modules]
678	  \label{def:schematic_support}
679	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.schematicSupport}
680	  \uses{def:modules_annihilator}
681	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
682	  % Polynomials" (read from
683	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
684	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
685	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
686	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
687	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
688	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$."
689	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
690	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
691	  \emph{schematic support} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the closed subscheme of \(X\)
692	  cut out by its annihilator ideal sheaf (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}):
693	  \[
694	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F})
695	    \;=\; V\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\bigr) \;\hookrightarrow\; X,
696	  \]
697	  equipped with its canonical closed immersion into \(X\). This realises
698	  Nitsure's ``schematic support of \(\mathcal{F}\)'' as a closed subscheme of
699	  \(X\).
700	\end{definition}

702	\begin{lemma}[Properness as a morphism property]
703	  \label{lem:isProper_mathlib}
704	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}
705	  \mathlibok
706	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
707	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper}).}
708	  A morphism \(f : X \to S\) of schemes is \emph{proper} iff it is separated,
709	  universally closed, and locally of finite type. Properness is stable under base
710	  change: if \(f : X \to S\) is proper and \(g : T \to S\) is any morphism then
711	  the base change \(X \times_S T \to T\) is proper. Consequently the base-change
712	  clause that Nitsure invokes (``properness \ldots\ preserved by base-change'')
713	  applies directly to the proper-support condition below.
714	\end{lemma}

716	\begin{definition}
717	[Proper support over the base]
718	  \label{def:has_proper_support}
719	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport}
720	  \uses{def:schematic_support, lem:isProper_mathlib}
721	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors $\Hilb_{X/S}$ and
722	  % $\Quot_{E/X/S}$" (read from
723	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L407-L409
724	  % + L418-L419).
725	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
726	  % "(i) a coherent sheaf $\F$ on $X_T = X\times_ST$
727	  %  such that the schematic support of $\F$ is proper over $T$
728	  %  and $\F$ is flat over $T$, together with
729	  %  [...]
730	  %  Then as properness and flatness are preserved
731	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,"
732	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
733	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
734	  \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism. We say \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{has proper support
735	  over \(S\) (along \(f\))} when the composite morphism
736	  \[
737	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F}) \hookrightarrow X \xrightarrow{f} S
738	  \]
739	  is proper (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}). Since properness is stable under base
740	  change (\cref{lem:isProper_mathlib}), this condition is preserved by pull-back
741	  along any \(S'\to S\), which is exactly what the well-definedness of the Quot
742	  functor's pullback action requires.
743	\end{definition}

1	# Analogy: QUOT predicate shapes — Mathlib idiom alignment

12	## Question
13	For the two project-side predicates needed by the Quot functor and Grassmannian
14	foundations — (1) "coherent sheaf with schematic support proper over the base" and
15	(2) "rank-`d` locally free `SheafOfModules`" — determine for each whether Mathlib
16	already supplies it, the recommended Mathlib-aligned Lean shape, the exact Mathlib
17	declarations to build on, and the cost of a parallel choice.
18	
19	## Project artifact(s)
20	- `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` `def:quot_functor` (L422-494) — the
21	  Quot functor classifies `T`-flat coherent quotients `q : E_T ↠ F` whose **schematic
22	  support is proper over `T`**; `def:grassmannian_scheme` (L504-564) classifies rank-`d`
23	  **locally free** quotients; `Grass(V,d)` is *defined* as `Quot^{d,O_S}_{V/S/S}`.
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean` — `hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`,
25	  `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable` are all typed `sorry` skeletons keyed on
26	  `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`; "coherent" is meant to be encoded as
27	  `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` + `[F.IsFiniteType]` (per `thm:generic_flatness`), but the
28	  proper-support and rank-`d`-local-freeness conditions are not yet expressed.

43	    — the **exact template** for an annihilator ideal sheaf.

69	### Decision 1b: How to express "support proper over `S`"
70	
71	- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`
72	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean:42`), a `MorphismProperty Scheme`
73	  `@[mk_iff] class IsProper extends IsSeparated, UniversallyClosed, LocallyOfFiniteType`,

116	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong if built as a parallel `IsLocallyFree` + a fabricated
117	  sheaf-level `rankAtStalk = d` conjunction (the sheaf-level `rankAtStalk` also does not
118	  exist, so that route doubles the gap-fill).
119	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL, but the *shape* is dictated by the existing idiom.
120	  Define a single `Prop` class
121	  `SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank (M : SheafOfModules R) (r : ℕ) : Prop`
122	  (or on `X.Modules`) witnessed by the existence of local data — an open cover on which `M`
123	  restricts to `free (Fin r)` — exactly paralleling `IsQuasicoherent`/`IsFinitePresentation`'s
124	  `Nonempty (local data)` shape and building on `SheafOfModules.free`. The rank is a
125	  parameter carried by the local model `free (Fin r)`, NOT a separate rank field; if the
126	  iso data is needed downstream, split it `…Data` (data) / `Is…` (Prop) as Mathlib does for
127	  `QuasicoherentData`/`IsQuasicoherent`. Expose `isLocallyFreeOfRank r : ObjectProperty …`
128	  for consistency.
129	
130	## Recommendation
131	
132	Both predicates are genuinely absent upstream, but neither is a free-form invention: each
133	must be assembled from existing Mathlib primitives along the established idiom.
134	
135	Predicate 1 = two layers. (i) **Support primitive** (gap-fill): mirror
136	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker`'s `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker …`
137	construction to define `Scheme.Modules.annihilator : X.Modules → X.IdealSheafData`
138	(`ofIdeals fun U ↦ Module.annihilator …`), giving `schematicSupport F := (F.annihilator).subscheme`
139	with the canonical closed immersion `(F.annihilator).subschemeι`. (ii) **Proper-over-S layer**
140	(align): `HasProperSupport f F := IsProper ((F.annihilator).subschemeι ≫ f)`, reusing
141	`AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` so `IsStableUnderBaseChange` discharges the base-change clause
142	the Quot functor's functoriality requires.
143	
144	Predicate 2: a single `Prop` class `IsLocallyFreeOfRank M r`, witnessed by `Nonempty` of
145	local-trivialization data against `SheafOfModules.free (Fin r)`, copying the
146	`IsQuasicoherent`/`IsFinitePresentation` pattern verbatim and reusing
147	`SheafOfModules.free`/`freeFunctor`/`mapFree`. Do not introduce a separate rank-agnostic
148	`IsLocallyFree` plus a sheaf-`rankAtStalk` conjunction (both would be new, and the param-on-
149	`free (Fin r)` form is the idiom).
150	
151	**Cost of a parallel choice.** Predicate 1: a bespoke set-only support loses the
152	closed-immersion instances (`subschemeι` is `IsPreimmersion`+`QuasiCompact` for free), the
153	`IdealSheafData.comap`/`comapIso` functoriality the Quot pullback action needs, and the
154	`IsProper` base-change instance — forcing hand-proved bridge lemmas for every base change in
155	the Quot functor. Predicate 2: a local-freeness not phrased through `free`/`freeFunctor`
156	cannot consume `mapFree`/`freeHomEquiv` for pulling back the tautological quotient, and —
157	because the blueprint *defines* `Grass(V,d) = Quot^{d,O_S}_{V/S/S}` — a parallel rank-`d`
158	predicate forces a bridge lemma `IsLocallyFreeOfRank d ↔ (flat ∧ properSupport ∧ hilbertPoly = d)`
159	on `X = S` to reconcile the Grassmannian's two descriptions.

771	\section{The Quot functor}
772	\label{sec:quot_functor}
773	
774	\begin{definition}\leanok
775	[Quot functor]
776	  \label{def:quot_functor}
777	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
778	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:has_proper_support}
779	
780	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) and
781	  % \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\)" (read from
782	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
783	  % + L481-L498).
784	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
785	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(X\to S\) be a finite type
786	  %  scheme over it. Let \(E\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X\).
787	  %  Let \(Sch_S\) denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
788	  %  over \(S\). For any \(T\to S\) in \(Sch_S\), a \textbf{family of quotients of \(E\)
789	  %  parametrised by \(T\)} will mean
790	  %  a pair \((\F,q)\) consisting of
791	  %
792	  %  (i) a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on \(X_T = X\times_ST\)
793	  %  such that the schematic support of \(\F\) is proper over \(T\)
794	  %  and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\), together with
795	  %
796	  %  (ii) a surjective \({\mathcal O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism of sheaves
797	  %  \(q:E_T  \to \F\) where \(E_T\) is the pull-back of \(E\) under
798	  %  the projection \(X_T \to X\).
799	  %
800	  %  Two such families \((\F,q)\) and \((\F,q)\) parametrised by \(T\) will
801	  %  be regarded as equivalent if \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\)),
802	  %  and \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) will denote
803	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
804	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
805	  %  the pull-back of \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) under an \(S\)-morphism \(T'\to T\) is
806	  %  well-defined, which gives
807	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
808	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets\) under which
809	  %  \(\)T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}\(\)
810	  %  [...]
811	  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes
812	  %  as a co-product
813	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
814	  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
815	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
816	  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
817	  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the
818	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction \(\F_t\), calculated
819	  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
820	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
821	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
822	  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
823	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
824	  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
825	  \[
826	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
827	  \]
828	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
829	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
830	  \begin{enumerate}
831	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
832	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
833	      which is flat over \(T\);
834	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
835	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
836	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
837	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
838	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
839	      \(\Phi\).
840	  \end{enumerate}
841	  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
842	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
843	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
844	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
845	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
846	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
847	\end{definition}

849	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
850	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
851	
852	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
853	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
854	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
855	linear algebra), we package the construction here.
856	
857	\begin{definition}\leanok
858	[Grassmannian scheme]
859	  \label{def:grassmannian_scheme}
860	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian}
861	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
862	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises~(2)" and
863	  % "Construction of Grassmannian" (read from
864	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L599
865	  % + L773-L850).
866	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
867	  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), an explicit construction
868	  %  the Grassmannian scheme
869	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
870	  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
871	  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
872	  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]
873	  %  Show that \(\grass(r,d)\) together with the quotient
874	  %  \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) represents the
875	  %  contravariant functor
876	  %  \(\)\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}\(\)
877	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any \(T\) the
878	  %  set of all equivalence classes
879	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients \(q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F\)
880	  %  where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\).
881	  %  [...]
882	  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if \(S\) is a scheme and
883	  %  \(E\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\),
884	  %  the functor \(\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}\)
885	  %  on all \(S\)-schemes which
886	  %  by definition associates to any \(T\)
887	  %  the set of all equivalence classes \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients
888	  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\),
889	  %  is representable. The representing scheme
890	  %  is denoted by \(\grass(E,d)\) and is called the rank \(d\)
891	  %  relative Grassmannian of \(E\) over \(S\)."
892	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
893	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
894	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
895	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
896	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
897	  \[
898	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
899	    \qquad
900	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
901	    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
902	    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
903	  \]
904	  with equivalence \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\); the rank-\(d\) local freeness of
905	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is as in \cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}. Concretely,
906	  \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) = \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\): the Quot functor
907	  (\cref{def:quot_functor}) in the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
908	  Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\).
909	\end{definition}

979	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(V\) a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module
980	  of rank \(r\), and \(1 \le d \le r\). The functor \(\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\) of

982	  \(S\)-scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\),
983	  equipped with a tautological quotient
984	  \(\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). The determinant
985	  line bundle \(\det(\mathcal{U})\) is relatively very ample, and the
986	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
987	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow

992	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
993	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
994	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
995	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
996	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
997	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
998	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
999	\begin{proof}
1000	  
1001	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
1002	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
1003	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
1004	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
1005	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
1006	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
1007	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
1008	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
1009	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
1010	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
1011	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
1012	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
1013	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
1014	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
1015	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
1016	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
1017	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
1018	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
1019	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
1020	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
1021	
1022	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
1023	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
1024	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The
1025	  determinant line bundle \(\det\mathcal{U}\) has transition functions
1026	  \(\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J\); the global sections
1027	  \(\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})\) defined by
1028	  \(\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I\) form a base-point-free linear system separating
1029	  points over \(\Spec\mathbb{Z}\), giving an embedding into
1030	  \(\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\), which is closed by
1031	  properness. Representability of \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)\) is then verified
1032	  on the universal pair \((\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)\): a quotient
1033	  \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of
1034	  rank \(d\) on \(T\) is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
1035	  the columns indexed by \(I\) are a basis the matrix of \(q\) has the form
1036	  \(X^I\), defining the classifying morphism \(T \to U^I \subset
1037	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Base change from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to a general noetherian
1038	  base \(S\) with a vector bundle \(V\) is direct: trivialise \(V\) on an open
1039	  cover and glue. For non-trivial \(V\) the same construction produces
1040	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) with Pl\"ucker embedding into
1041	  \(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\).
1042	\end{proof}
1043	
1044	\section{Out of scope}
1045	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
1046	
1047	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-foundations layer: the Hilbert
1048	polynomial, the Quot functor, and the Grassmannian scheme with its
1049	representability. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
1050	\begin{itemize}
1051	  \item \textbf{Representability of the Quot scheme} -- the
1052	    Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman existence theorem and its proof skeleton
1053	    (boundedness, Grassmannian embedding, flattening stratification,
1054	    valuative criterion) are not constructed in this chapter.
1055	  \item \textbf{Flat base change of cohomology} (Stacks 02KH) and its
1056	    project-side substrate -- not constructed in this chapter.
1057	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
1058	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
1059	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot functor and is
1060	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
1061	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
1062	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
1063	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
1064	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
1065	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
1066	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
1067	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
1068	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)} (A.2.c) -- the
1069	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream. Its blueprint home is the
1070	    FGA Picard-representability assembly (the chapter that bundles the relative
1071	    Picard functor, its representability, and the abelian-group-scheme
1072	    structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)); that assembly lives in the parent project
1073	    which consumes this Quot-foundations layer and is not part of the present
1074	    subproject.
1075	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety
1076	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
1077	    constructed in this chapter.
1078	\end{itemize}

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
32	
33	variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52	
53	section Functor
54	
55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
79	
80	end Functor

82	variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}
83	
84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule
93	
94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119	
120	@[ext]
121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)
125	
126	lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M
127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]
136	
137	instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _
138	
139	@[simp, push ←]
140	lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
141	  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
142	  simp [← Hom.comp_app]
143	
144	end Presheaf

146	noncomputable section Functorial
147	
148	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)
149	
150	/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
151	def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
152	  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom
153	
154	@[simp]
155	lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
156	    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
157	
158	@[simp]
159	lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
160	    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl
161	
162	@[simp]
163	lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
164	    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl
165	
166	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
167	def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
168	  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom
169	
170	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
171	is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
172	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
173	  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _
174	
175	section
176	
177	attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
178	  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts
179	
180	instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
181	instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
182	instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
183	instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
184	
185	end
186	
187	variable (X) in
188	/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
189	to the identity functor. -/
190	def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
191	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
192	
193	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
194	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
195	
196	variable (X) in
197	/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
198	to the identity functor. -/
199	def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
200	  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _
201	
202	variable (X) in
203	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
204	    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
205	      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
206	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _
207	
208	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
209	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
210	def pushforwardComp :
211	    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
212	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _
213	
214	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
215	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
216	
217	/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
218	identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
219	def pullbackComp :
220	    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
221	  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _
222	
223	/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
224	def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
225	    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
226	      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
227	
228	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
229	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
230	
231	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
232	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl
233	
234	/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
235	def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
236	  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)
237	
238	lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
239	    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
240	      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
241	    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
242	  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _
243	
244	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
245	@[reassoc]
246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
247	    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
248	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
250	    eqToHom (by simp) := by
251	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
252	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
258	      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
259	  simp [this]
260	
261	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
262	@[reassoc]
263	lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
264	    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
265	      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
266	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
267	  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
268	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
269	  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
270	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
271	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
272	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
273	  simp [← this]
274	
275	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
276	@[reassoc]
277	lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
278	    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
279	      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
280	        eqToHom (by simp) := by
281	  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
282	  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
283	  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
284	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
285	  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
286	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
287	  simp [← this]
288	
289	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
290	attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
291	  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
292	  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
293	/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
294	a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
295	(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
296	these categories.) -/
297	@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
298	  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
299	  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
300	def pseudofunctor :
301	    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
302	  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
303	    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
304	    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
305	    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
306	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
307	    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
308	        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))
309	
310	end Functorial
311	
312	noncomputable section Restriction
313	
314	variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]
315	
316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
321	  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
322	    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩
323	
324	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
325	abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
326	  (restrictFunctor f).obj M
327	
328	@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
329	    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl
330	
331	@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333	
334	/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x
348	    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
350	      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
351	    exact congr($this x)
352	
353	instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
354	  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)
355	
356	instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
357	instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
358	instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
359	
360	@[simp]
361	lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
362	    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
363	      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl
364	
365	@[simp]
366	lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
367	    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
368	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl
369	
370	/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
371	def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
372	  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
373	
374	/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
375	def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
376	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
377	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
378	      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
379	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
380	
381	@[simp]
382	lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
383	    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
384	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl
385	
386	@[simp]
387	lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
388	    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
389	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl
390	
391	/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
392	def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
393	  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
394	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
395	    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
396	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
397	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
398	    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm
399	
400	@[simp]
401	lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
402	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
403	
404	@[simp]
405	lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
406	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
407	
408	/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
409	def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
410	    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
411	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
412	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)
413	
414	@[simp]
415	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
416	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
417	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
418	
419	@[simp]
420	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
421	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
422	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
423	
424	/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
425	def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
426	    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
427	    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
428	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
429	  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
430	      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
431	      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)
432	
433	@[simp]
434	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
435	    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
436	      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
437	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
438	  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
439	    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
440	    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _
441	
442	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
443	@[simp]
444	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
445	    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
446	      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
447	  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
448	    Iso.hom_inv_id]
449	  simp
450	
451	end Restriction
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

16	/-!
17	
18	# Construction of M^~
19	
20	Given any commutative ring `R` and `R`-module `M`, we construct the sheaf `M^~` of `𝒪_SpecR`-modules
21	such that `M^~(U)` is the set of dependent functions that are locally fractions.
22	
23	## Main definitions
24	* `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` : `M^~` as a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules.
25	* `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction` : `~` is left adjoint to taking global sections.
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public noncomputable section
30	
31	universe u
32	
33	open TopCat AlgebraicGeometry TopologicalSpace CategoryTheory Opposite
34	
35	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	open _root_.PrimeSpectrum
40	
41	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules to sheaves of `R`-modules. -/
42	def modulesSpecToSheaf :
43	    (Spec R).Modules ⥤ TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat R) (Spec R) :=
44	  SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (.op ⊤)
45	    (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop) ⋙
46	  sheafCompose _ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso R).hom.hom)
47	
48	/-- The global section functor for `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules -/
49	noncomputable
50	def moduleSpecΓFunctor : (Spec (.of R)).Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R :=
51	  modulesSpecToSheaf ⋙ TopCat.Sheaf.forget _ _ ⋙ (evaluation _ _).obj (.op ⊤)
52	
53	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
54	open PrimeSpectrum in
55	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules to sheaves of `R`-modules is fully faithful. -/
56	def SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful : (modulesSpecToSheaf (R := R)).FullyFaithful where
57	  preimage {M N} f := ⟨fun U ↦ ModuleCat.ofHom ⟨(f.1.app U).hom.toAddHom, by
58	    intro t m
59	    apply TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.section_ext (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).2
60	    intro x hxU
61	    obtain ⟨a, ⟨_, ⟨r, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, hxr, hrU : basicOpen _ ≤ _⟩ :=
62	      PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxU U.unop.2
63	    refine ⟨_, hrU, hxr, ?_⟩
64	    refine Eq.trans ?_ (N.val.map_smul (homOfLE hrU).op t _).symm
65	    change N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ _) = _ • N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ _)
66	    have (x : _) :
67	        f.1.app _ (M.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op _) = N.1.map (homOfLE hrU).op (f.1.app _ x) :=
68	      congr($(f.1.naturality (homOfLE hrU).op).hom x)
69	    rw [← this, ← this, M.val.map_smul]
70	    generalize (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE hrU).op t = t
71	    letI := Module.compHom (R := Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r)) Γ(M, basicOpen r)
72	      (algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r))
73	    haveI : IsScalarTower R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(M, basicOpen r) :=
74	      .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ ↦ rfl
75	    letI := Module.compHom Γ(N, basicOpen r) (algebraMap R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r))
76	    haveI : IsScalarTower R Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(N, basicOpen r) :=
77	      .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ ↦ rfl
78	    exact (IsLocalization.linearMap_compatibleSMul (.powers (M := R) r)
79	      Γ(Spec R, basicOpen r) Γ(M, basicOpen r) Γ(N, basicOpen r)).map_smul
80	      (f.hom.app _).hom _ _⟩, fun i ↦ by ext x; exact congr($(f.1.naturality i).hom x)⟩
81	  map_preimage f := rfl
82	  preimage_map f := rfl
83	
84	/--
85	`M^~` as a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules
86	-/
87	def tilde : (Spec R).Modules where
88	  val := moduleStructurePresheaf R M
89	  isSheaf := (TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp (forget AddCommGrpCat) _).2
90	    (structureSheafInType R M).2
91	
92	namespace tilde
93	
94	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
95	/-- (Implementation). The image of `tilde` under `modulesSpecToSheaf` is isomorphic to
96	`structurePresheafInModuleCat`. They are defeq as types but the `Smul` instance are not defeq. -/
97	noncomputable
98	def modulesSpecToSheafIso :
99	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).1 ≅ structurePresheafInModuleCat R M :=
100	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun U ↦ LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
101	    (X₁ := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj _)
102	    { __ := AddEquiv.refl _,
103	      map_smul' r m := IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (M := ((structureSheafInType R M).obj.obj U))
104	        ((structureSheafInType R R).obj.obj U) r m }) fun _ ↦ rfl
105	
106	/-- The map from `M` to `Γ(M, U)`. This is a localization map when `U = D(f)`. -/
107	def toOpen (U : (Spec R).Opens) : M ⟶ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj (.op U) :=
108	  ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M U) ≫ ((modulesSpecToSheafIso M).app _).inv
109	
110	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
111	theorem toOpen_res (U V : Opens (PrimeSpectrum.Top R)) (i : V ⟶ U) :
112	    toOpen M U ≫ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op = toOpen M V :=
113	  rfl
114	
115	instance (f : R) : IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (toOpen M (basicOpen f)).hom :=
116	  .of_linearEquiv (.powers f) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M (basicOpen f))
117	    ((modulesSpecToSheafIso M).app _).toLinearEquiv.symm
118	
119	noncomputable
120	instance (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) : Module R ((tilde M).presheaf.stalk x) :=
121	  inferInstanceAs (Module R ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf x))
122	
123	/--
124	If `x` is a point of `Spec R`, this is the morphism of `R`-modules from `M` to the stalk of
125	`M^~` at `x`.
126	-/
127	noncomputable def toStalk (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
128	    ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ ModuleCat.of R ((tilde M).presheaf.stalk x) :=
129	  ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.toStalkₗ ..)
130	
131	instance (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
132	    IsLocalizedModule x.asIdeal.primeCompl (toStalk M x).hom :=
133	  inferInstanceAs (IsLocalizedModule x.asIdeal.primeCompl (StructureSheaf.toStalkₗ ..))
134	
135	/-- The tilde construction is functorial. -/
136	protected noncomputable def map {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) : tilde M ⟶ tilde N :=
137	  SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.preimage ⟨(modulesSpecToSheafIso M).hom ≫
138	    { app U := ModuleCat.ofHom (StructureSheaf.comapₗ f.hom _ _ .rfl) } ≫
139	    (modulesSpecToSheafIso N).inv⟩
140	
141	@[simp, reassoc]
142	protected lemma map_id {M : ModuleCat R} : tilde.map (𝟙 M) = 𝟙 _ := by
143	  ext p x
144	  exact Subtype.ext (funext fun y ↦ DFunLike.congr_fun (LocalizedModule.map_id _) _)
145	
146	@[simp, reassoc]
147	protected lemma map_comp {M N P : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) :
148	    tilde.map (f ≫ g) = tilde.map f ≫ tilde.map g := by
149	  ext p x
150	  exact Subtype.ext (funext
151	    fun y ↦ DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.map_comp' y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl
152	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl M)
153	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl N)
154	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl P) _ _) _)
155	
156	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
157	lemma toOpen_map_app {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N)
158	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens (PrimeSpectrum R)) :
159	    toOpen M U ≫ (modulesSpecToSheaf.map (tilde.map f)).1.app _ =
160	    f ≫ toOpen N U := by
161	  ext x; exact Subtype.ext (funext fun y ↦ IsLocalizedModule.map_apply y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl
162	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl M)
163	     (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap y.1.asIdeal.primeCompl N) _ x)
164	
165	variable (R) in
166	/-- Tilde as a functor -/
167	@[simps] protected noncomputable def functor : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec (.of R)).Modules where
168	  obj := tilde
169	  map := tilde.map
170	
171	instance isIso_toOpen_top {M : ModuleCat R} : IsIso (toOpen M ⊤) := by
172	  rw [toOpen, isIso_comp_right_iff, ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
173	  exact StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ_top_bijective
174	
175	/-- The isomorphism between the global sections of `M^~` and `M`. -/
176	@[simps! hom]
177	noncomputable def isoTop (M : ModuleCat R) :
178	    M ≅ (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤) :=
179	  asIso (toOpen M ⊤)
180	
181	open PrimeSpectrum in
182	lemma isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) (f : R) :
183	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R
184	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op (basicOpen f)))) f) := by
185	  rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
186	  change Function.Bijective (algebraMap Γ(Spec R, basicOpen f)
187	      (Module.End Γ(Spec R, basicOpen f) Γ(M, basicOpen f)) (algebraMap R _ f))
188	  rw [← Module.End.isUnit_iff]
189	  exact (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _).map _
190	
191	end tilde

193	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
194	/-- This is the counit of the tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
195	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) :
196	    tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) ⟶ M :=
197	  SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.preimage
198	    ⟨TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ isBasis_basic_opens
199	    { app (f : Rᵒᵖ) := by
200	        refine (ModuleCat.ofHom (IsLocalizedModule.lift (.powers (M := R) f.unop)
201	          (tilde.toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f.unop)).hom
202	          ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom ?_):)
203	        rw [Subtype.forall]
204	        change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
205	        simp only [inducedFunctor_obj, Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap]
206	        exact tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M f.unop
207	      naturality {f g : Rᵒᵖ} i := by
208	        letI N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)
209	        ext1
210	        apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers (M := R) f.unop)
211	          (tilde.toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) f.unop)).hom
212	        · rw [Subtype.forall]
213	          change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
214	          simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
215	          obtain ⟨n, a, e⟩ : ∃ n, f.unop ∣ g.unop ^ n := by
216	            simpa only [Ideal.mem_radical_iff, Ideal.mem_span_singleton] using
217	              (basicOpen_le_basicOpen_iff _ _).mp (i.1.hom.le)
218	          refine ((Commute.isUnit_mul_iff (b := algebraMap R _ a) (.map (.all _ _) _)).mp ?_).1
219	          rw [← map_mul, ← e, map_pow]
220	          exact (tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M g.unop).pow n
221	        · dsimp [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]
222	          rw [tilde.toOpen_res_assoc]
223	          ext x
224	          dsimp
225	          simp only [IsLocalizedModule.lift_apply, ← ModuleCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
226	          rfl }⟩

228	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
229	@[reassoc]
230	lemma Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app (M : (Spec (.of R)).Modules) (U) :
231	    tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) U ≫
232	      (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ).1.app (.op U) =
233	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).1.map (homOfLE le_top).op := by
234	  wlog hU : U = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen 1 generalizing U
235	  · rw [← tilde.toOpen_res _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen 1) _ (homOfLE (by simp)), Category.assoc,
236	      NatTrans.naturality, ← Category.assoc, this, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, homOfLE_comp]
237	    simp
238	  subst hU
239	  simp only [fromTildeΓ, inducedFunctor_obj,
240	    homOfLE_leOfHom, Functor.FullyFaithful.map_preimage, TopCat.Sheaf.extend_hom_app]
241	  ext x
242	  refine (IsLocalizedModule.lift_apply (.powers (M := R) 1)
243	    (tilde.toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) 1)).hom
244	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom (by simp) x)
245	
246	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
247	/-- This is the counit of the tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
248	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓNatTrans :
249	    moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ⋙ tilde.functor (R := R) ⟶ 𝟭 _ where
250	  app := fromTildeΓ
251	  naturality {M N} f := by
252	    apply SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.map_injective
253	    apply CategoryTheory.Sheaf.hom_ext
254	    apply (TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens).symm.injective
255	    ext r : 3
256	    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers (M := R) r.unop)
257	      (tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤))
258	        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) r.unop)).hom
259	    · rw [Subtype.forall]
260	      change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
261	      simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
262	      exact tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen ..
263	    dsimp [TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom, Functor.IsCoverDense.restrictHomEquivHom,
264	      moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget]
265	    simp only [← ModuleCat.hom_comp, Functor.map_comp]
266	    congr 1
267	    erw [tilde.toOpen_map_app_assoc, toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r.unop),
268	      toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app_assoc M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r.unop),
269	      ← (modulesSpecToSheaf.map f).hom.naturality]
270	
271	/-- `tilde.isoTop` bundled as a natural isomorphism.
272	This is the unit of the tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
273	def tilde.toTildeΓNatIso : 𝟭 _ ≅ tilde.functor R ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor :=
274	  NatIso.ofComponents tilde.isoTop fun f ↦ (tilde.toOpen_map_app f _).symm
275	
276	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
277	open Scheme.Modules in
278	/-- The tilde-Gamma adjunction. -/
279	def tilde.adjunction : tilde.functor R ⊣ moduleSpecΓFunctor where
280	  unit := toTildeΓNatIso.hom
281	  counit := fromTildeΓNatTrans
282	  left_triangle_components M := by
283	    apply SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.map_injective
284	    apply CategoryTheory.Sheaf.hom_ext
285	    apply (TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens).symm.injective
286	    ext r : 3
287	    apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (.powers (M := R) r.unop)
288	      (toOpen _ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (R := R) r.unop)).hom
289	    · rw [Subtype.forall]
290	      change Submonoid.powers _ ≤ (IsUnit.submonoid _).comap _
291	      simp only [Submonoid.powers_le, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff]
292	      exact isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen ..
293	    dsimp [toTildeΓNatIso, isoTop,
294	      TopCat.Sheaf.restrictHomEquivHom, Functor.IsCoverDense.restrictHomEquivHom,
295	      fromTildeΓNatTrans, moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget, sheafToPresheaf]
296	    simp only [← ModuleCat.hom_comp, Functor.map_comp]
297	    congr 1
298	    rw [ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.comp_hom]
299	    dsimp
300	    rw [toOpen_map_app_assoc, toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app]
301	    rfl
302	  right_triangle_components M := by
303	    dsimp [toTildeΓNatIso, fromTildeΓNatTrans, tilde.isoTop, moduleSpecΓFunctor, Sheaf.forget]
304	    rw [toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app]
305	    exact (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).obj.map_id _
306	
307	instance : IsIso (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).unit := by
308	  dsimp [tilde.adjunction]; infer_instance
309	
310	/-- The tilde functor is fully faithful. We will later show that the essential image is
311	exactly quasi-coherent modules. -/
312	def tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor : (tilde.functor R).FullyFaithful :=
313	  tilde.adjunction.fullyFaithfulLOfIsIsoUnit
314	
315	instance : (tilde.functor R).Full := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.full
316	instance : (tilde.functor R).Faithful := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.faithful
317	instance : (tilde.functor R).IsLeftAdjoint := tilde.adjunction.isLeftAdjoint
318	instance : (tilde.functor R).Additive :=
319	  have := Limits.preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts (tilde.functor R)
320	  Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
321	
322	section
323	
324	variable {M N : ModuleCat R} (f g : M ⟶ N)
325	
326	@[simp] lemma tilde.map_zero : tilde.map (0 : M ⟶ N) = 0 :=
327	  (tilde.functor R).map_zero _ _
328	
329	@[simp] lemma tilde.map_add : tilde.map (f + g) = tilde.map f + tilde.map g :=
330	  (tilde.functor R).map_add
331	
332	@[simp] lemma tilde.map_sub : tilde.map (f - g) = tilde.map f - tilde.map g :=
333	  (tilde.functor R).map_sub
334	
335	@[simp] lemma tilde.map_neg : tilde.map (-f) = - tilde.map f :=
336	  (tilde.functor R).map_neg
337	
338	end
339	
340	lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff {M : (Spec R).Modules} :
341	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage M :=
342	  tilde.adjunction.isIso_counit_app_iff_mem_essImage

344	section IsQuasicoherent
345	
346	open Limits
347	
348	/-- Tilde of `R` as an `R`-module is isomorphic to the structure sheaf `𝒪_{Spec R}`. -/
349	noncomputable
350	def tildeSelf : tilde (ModuleCat.of R R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit.{u} _ := .refl _
351	
352	instance : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (SheafOfModules.unit.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)) :=
353	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff.mpr ⟨_, ⟨tildeSelf⟩⟩
354	
355	/-- Tilde of direct sums of `R` as an `R`-module is isomorphic to the free sheaf. -/
356	noncomputable
357	def tildeFinsupp (ι : Type u) : tilde (ModuleCat.of R (ι →₀ R)) ≅ SheafOfModules.free.{u} ι :=
358	  letI H : IsColimit <| (tilde.functor R).mapCocone (ModuleCat.finsuppCocone R R ι) :=
359	    isColimitOfPreserves (tilde.functor R) (ModuleCat.finsuppCoconeIsColimit R R ι)
360	  letI iso : (Discrete.functor fun (_ : ι) ↦ ModuleCat.of R R) ⋙ tilde.functor R ≅
361	         Discrete.functor fun _ ↦ SheafOfModules.unit.{u} _ :=
362	      Discrete.natIso (fun _ ↦ tildeSelf)
363	  IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso
364	    ((IsColimit.precomposeHomEquiv iso.symm _).symm H) (coproductIsCoproduct _)
365	
366	instance (ι : Type u) :
367	    IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ (R := R) (SheafOfModules.free.{u} ι)) :=
368	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff.mpr ⟨_, ⟨tildeFinsupp _⟩⟩
369	
370	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
371	/-- Given a presentation of a module `M`, we may construct an associated presentation of `M^~`. -/
372	noncomputable
373	def presentationTilde (s : Set M) (hs : Submodule.span R s = ⊤)
374	    (t : Set (s →₀ R))
375	    (ht : Submodule.span R t = LinearMap.ker (Finsupp.linearCombination R ((↑) : s → M))) :
376	    (tilde M).Presentation := by
377	  haveI H₁ : Function.Exact
378	      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := t) R (↑)))
379	      (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination (α := s) (M := M) R (↑))) :=
380	    (LinearMap.exact_iff.mpr (by simp [Finsupp.range_linearCombination, ht]))
381	  refine SheafOfModules.presentationOfIsCokernelFree.{u}
382	      ((tildeFinsupp t).inv ≫ tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑))) ≫
383	        (tildeFinsupp s).hom) ((tildeFinsupp s).inv ≫
384	          tilde.map (ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.linearCombination R (↑)))) (by
385	    simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, Preadditive.IsIso.comp_left_eq_zero]
386	    rw [← tilde.map_comp, ← ModuleCat.ofHom_comp]
387	    convert tilde.map_zero
388	    exact congr(ModuleCat.ofHom $(H₁.linearMap_comp_eq_zero))) ?_
389	  letI h₁ := ModuleCat.isColimitCokernelCofork _ _ H₁
390	    (by simp [← LinearMap.range_eq_top, Finsupp.range_linearCombination, hs])
391	  refine IsCokernel.ofIso _ (CokernelCofork.mapIsColimit _ h₁ (tilde.functor R)) _ (tildeFinsupp t)
392	    (tildeFinsupp s) (.refl _) (by simp) (by simp)
393	
394	instance : (tilde M).IsQuasicoherent :=
395	  (presentationTilde.{u} _ .univ (by simp) _ (Submodule.span_eq _)).isQuasicoherent
396	
397	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
398	lemma isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation (M : (Spec R).Modules) (P : M.Presentation) :
399	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
400	  rw [isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff]
401	  let g := (tilde.functor _).preimage <| (tildeFinsupp _).hom ≫ P.relations.π ≫ kernel.ι _ ≫
402	    (tildeFinsupp _).inv
403	  let iso : cokernel ((tilde.functor R).map g) ≅ cokernel (P.relations.π ≫ kernel.ι _) := by
404	    refine cokernel.mapIso _ _ (tildeFinsupp _) (tildeFinsupp _) ?_
405	    simp only [g, (tilde.functor R).map_preimage]
406	    simp
407	  exact ⟨cokernel g, ⟨PreservesCokernel.iso (tilde.functor R) g ≪≫ iso ≪≫
408	    IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _) P.isColimit⟩⟩
409	
410	end IsQuasicoherent
411	
412	end AlgebraicGeometry

418	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-11")] alias Tilde.toOpen_res := tilde.toOpen_res
