12	/-!
13	# Quasicoherent sheaves
14	
15	A sheaf of modules is quasi-coherent if it admits locally a presentation as the
16	cokernel of a morphism between coproducts of copies of the sheaf of rings.
17	When these coproducts are finite, we say that the sheaf is of finite presentation.
18	
19	## References
20	
21	* https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01BD
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe w u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
32	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
33	
34	namespace SheafOfModules
35	
36	section
37	
38	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
39	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
40	
41	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of modules `M` consists of a family `generators.s`
42	of sections `s` which generate `M`, and a family of sections which generate
43	the kernel of the morphism `generators.π : free (generators.I) ⟶ M`. -/
44	structure Presentation (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
45	  /-- generators -/
46	  generators : M.GeneratingSections
47	  /-- relations -/
48	  relations : (kernel generators.π).GeneratingSections
49	
50	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of module if finite if the type
51	of generators and relations are finite. -/
52	class Presentation.IsFinite {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation) : Prop where
53	  isFiniteType_generators : p.generators.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
54	  isFiniteType_relations : p.relations.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
55	
56	attribute [instance] Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_generators
57	  Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations
58	
59	@[deprecated Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations (since := "2026-04-14")]
60	lemma Presentation.IsFinite.finite_relations {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation)
61	    [p.IsFinite] : Finite p.relations.I := GeneratingSections.IsFiniteType.finite
62	
63	end
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
68	  [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
69	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)] {ι σ : Type u}
70	
71	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
72	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
73	generators of `Presentation M`. -/
74	@[simps! I s]
75	def generatorsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
76	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
77	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : M.GeneratingSections where
78	  I := σ
79	  s := M.freeHomEquiv g
80	  epi := by simpa using epi_of_isColimit_cofork H'
81	
82	@[simp]
83	theorem generatorsOfIsCokernelFree_π {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
84	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
85	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) :
86	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π = g := M.freeHomEquiv.symm_apply_apply g
87	
88	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
89	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
90	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
91	relations of `Presentation M`. -/
92	@[simps! I s]
93	def relationsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
94	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
95	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) :
96	    (kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π).GeneratingSections where
97	  I := ι
98	  s := (kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π).freeHomEquiv <| kernel.lift
99	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])
100	  epi := by
101	    let h : cokernel f ≅ M := (H'.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _)).symm
102	    let h' : Abelian.image f ≅ kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π :=
103	      kernel.mapIso (cokernel.π f) (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π
104	        (Iso.refl _) h (by simp [h])
105	    have comp_aux : Abelian.factorThruImage f ≫ h'.hom =
106	      (kernel.lift (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])) :=
107	        equalizer.hom_ext <| by simp [h']
108	    rw [← comp_aux, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
109	    infer_instance
110	
111	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
112	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain a
113	`Presentation M`. -/
114	@[simps]
115	def presentationOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
116	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
117	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : Presentation M where
118	  generators := generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
119	  relations := relationsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
120	
121	/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, there is two morphism of
122	sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M` satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0`
123	and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`. -/
124	def Presentation.isColimit {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
125	    IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ (f := (freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s ≫ (kernel.ι _))
126	      P.generators.π (by simp)) :=
127	  isCokernelEpiComp (c := CokernelCofork.ofπ _ (kernel.condition P.generators.π))
128	      (Abelian.epiIsCokernelOfKernel _ <| limit.isLimit _) _ rfl
129	
130	/-- Mapping a presentation under an isomorphism. -/
131	@[simps]
132	noncomputable def Presentation.ofIsIso {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
133	    (σ : M.Presentation) : N.Presentation where
134	  generators := σ.generators.ofEpi f
135	  relations := σ.relations.ofEpi ((kernelCompMono _ f).symm.trans <| eqToIso (by simp)).hom
136	
137	@[deprecated (since := "2026-04-15")] alias Presentation.of_isIso := Presentation.ofIsIso
138	
139	instance {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
140	    (σ : M.Presentation) [σ.IsFinite] : (σ.ofIsIso f).IsFinite where
141	  isFiniteType_generators := inferInstanceAs (σ.generators.ofEpi _).IsFiniteType
142	  isFiniteType_relations := inferInstanceAs (σ.relations.ofEpi _).IsFiniteType
143	
144	variable {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} C'] {J' : GrothendieckTopology C'} {S : Sheaf J' RingCat.{u}}
145	  [HasSheafify J' AddCommGrpCat] [J'.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
146	  [J'.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
147	
148	variable {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M)
149	  (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S) [PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F]
150	  (η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S)
151	
152	-- `preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink` is not a global instance because it loops indefinitely.
153	-- But here it is fine as an instance since the universe `u` is inferrable from the type of `F`.
154	local instance : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{0, 0} F := preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink _
155	
156	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
157	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will obtain
158	relations of `Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
159	def Presentation.mapRelations : free P.relations.I (R := S) ⟶ free P.generators.I :=
160	  (mapFree F η P.relations.I).inv ≫ F.map ((freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s) ≫
161	    F.map (kernel.ι _) ≫ (mapFree F η P.generators.I).hom
162	
163	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
164	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will obtain
165	generators of `Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
166	def Presentation.mapGenerators : free P.generators.I ⟶ F.obj M :=
167	  (mapFree F η P.generators.I).inv ≫ F.map (P.generators.π)
168	
169	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
170	theorem Presentation.mapRelations_mapGenerators :
171	    P.mapRelations F η ≫ P.mapGenerators F η = 0 := by
172	  simp only [mapRelations, mapGenerators, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc,
173	    ← Functor.map_comp, kernel.condition, Functor.map_zero, comp_zero]
174	
175	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
176	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will get a
177	`Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
178	@[simps! generators_I relations_I]
179	def Presentation.map : Presentation (F.obj M) :=
180	  presentationOfIsCokernelFree (P.mapRelations F η) (P.mapGenerators F η)
181	    (P.mapRelations_mapGenerators F η) <| by
182	    refine IsColimit.equivOfNatIsoOfIso (parallelPairIsoMk (mapFree F η _) (mapFree F η _)
183	      (by simp [Presentation.mapRelations]) (by simp)) _ _ ?_ (isColimitOfPreserves F P.isColimit)
184	    exact (Cocone.ext (Iso.refl _) <| by rintro (_ | _)
185	      <;> simp [Presentation.mapRelations, Presentation.mapGenerators, ← Functor.map_comp])
186	
187	theorem Presentation.map_π_eq :
188	    (P.map F η).generators.π = (mapFree F η _).inv ≫ F.map (P.generators.π) :=
189	  (F.obj M).freeHomEquiv.symm_apply_eq.mpr rfl
190	
191	end
192	
193	section
194	
195	variable [∀ X, (J.over X).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
196	  [∀ X, HasWeakSheafify (J.over X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
197	  [∀ X, (J.over X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
198	
199	/-- This structure contains the data of a family of objects `X i` which cover
200	the terminal object, and of a presentation of `M.over (X i)` for all `i`. -/
201	structure QuasicoherentData (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
202	  /-- the index type of the covering -/
203	  I : Type w
204	  /-- a family of objects which cover the terminal object -/
205	  X : I → C
206	  coversTop : J.CoversTop X
207	  /-- a presentation of the sheaf of modules `M.over (X i)` for any `i : I` -/
208	  presentation (i : I) : (M.over (X i)).Presentation
209	
210	namespace QuasicoherentData
211	
212	/-- Shrink the indexing type of `QuasicoherentData` into the universe of the site. -/
213	noncomputable
214	def shrink {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
215	    QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M where
216	  I := Set.range q.X
217	  X i := q.X i.2.choose
218	  coversTop X := by
219	    refine J.superset_covering (fun Y hY H ↦ ?_) (q.coversTop X)
220	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hi⟩⟩ := (Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff ..).mp H
221	    exact ⟨⟨_, i, rfl⟩, ⟨hi ≫ eqToHom (by grind)⟩⟩
222	  presentation i := q.presentation i.2.choose
223	
224	/-- If `M` is quasicoherent, it is locally generated by sections. -/
225	@[simps]
226	def localGeneratorsData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
227	    M.LocalGeneratorsData where
228	  I := q.I
229	  X := q.X
230	  coversTop := q.coversTop
231	  generators i := (q.presentation i).generators
232	
233	/-- A (local) presentation of a sheaf of module `M` is a finite presentation
234	if each given presentation of `M.over (X i)` is a finite presentation. -/
235	class IsFinitePresentation {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) : Prop where
236	  isFinite_presentation (i : q.I) : (q.presentation i).IsFinite := by infer_instance
237	
238	attribute [instance] IsFinitePresentation.isFinite_presentation
239	
240	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
241	instance {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) [q.IsFinitePresentation] :
242	    q.localGeneratorsData.IsFiniteType where
243	  isFiniteType := by dsimp; infer_instance
244	
245	end QuasicoherentData
246	
247	/-- A sheaf of modules is quasi-coherent if it is locally the cokernel of a
248	morphism between coproducts of copies of the sheaf of rings. -/
249	class IsQuasicoherent (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) : Prop where
250	  nonempty_quasicoherentData : Nonempty (QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M) := by infer_instance
251	
252	lemma QuasicoherentData.isQuasicoherent {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
253	    M.IsQuasicoherent := ⟨⟨q.shrink⟩⟩
254	
255	variable (R) in
256	@[inherit_doc IsQuasicoherent]
257	abbrev isQuasicoherent : ObjectProperty (SheafOfModules.{u} R) :=
258	  IsQuasicoherent
259	
260	/-- A sheaf of modules is finitely presented if it is locally the cokernel of a
261	morphism between coproducts of finitely many copies of the sheaf of rings. -/
262	class IsFinitePresentation (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) : Prop where
263	  exists_quasicoherentData (M) :
264	    ∃ (σ : QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M), σ.IsFinitePresentation
265	
266	variable (R) in
267	@[inherit_doc IsFinitePresentation]
268	abbrev isFinitePresentation : ObjectProperty (SheafOfModules.{u} R) :=
269	  IsFinitePresentation
270	
271	instance (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) [M.IsFinitePresentation] :
272	    M.IsQuasicoherent where
273	  nonempty_quasicoherentData :=
274	    ⟨(IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData M).choose⟩
275	
276	instance (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) [M.IsFinitePresentation] :
277	    M.IsFiniteType where
278	  exists_localGeneratorsData := by
279	    obtain ⟨σ, _⟩ := IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData M
280	    exact ⟨σ.localGeneratorsData, inferInstance⟩
281	
282	/-- A choice of local presentations when `M` is a sheaf of modules of finite presentation. -/
283	@[deprecated "Use the lemma `IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData` instead."
284	  (since := "2025-10-28")]
285	noncomputable def quasicoherentDataOfIsFinitePresentation
286	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) [M.IsFinitePresentation] : M.QuasicoherentData :=
287	  (IsFinitePresentation.exists_quasicoherentData M).choose
288	
289	end

291	noncomputable section
292	
293	open CategoryTheory Limits
294	
295	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] [HasBinaryProducts C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
296	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
297	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
298	
299	variable [∀ X, (J.over X).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
300	  [∀ X, HasSheafify (J.over X) AddCommGrpCat]
301	  [∀ X, (J.over X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
302	
303	/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, we may construct the quasi-coherent
304	data on the trivial cover. -/
305	@[simps]
306	def Presentation.quasicoherentData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
307	    QuasicoherentData M where
308	  I := C
309	  X := id
310	  coversTop x := GrothendieckTopology.covering_of_eq_top J <| by
311	    rw [Sieve.ext_iff]
312	    intro _ f
313	    simpa [Sieve.top_apply, iff_true] using ⟨x, Nonempty.intro f⟩
314	  presentation x := P.map (pushforward (𝟙 (R.over x))) (by rfl)
315	
316	/-- If a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` has a presentation, then `M` is quasi-coherent. -/
317	theorem Presentation.isQuasicoherent {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
318	    IsQuasicoherent M where
319	  nonempty_quasicoherentData := Nonempty.intro (Presentation.quasicoherentData P)
320	
321	/-- Mapping quasicoherent data under an isomorphism. -/
322	@[simps]
323	noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.ofIsIso {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
324	    (σ : M.QuasicoherentData) : N.QuasicoherentData where
325	  I := σ.I
326	  X := σ.X
327	  coversTop := σ.coversTop
328	  presentation i := Presentation.ofIsIso (f.over (σ.X i)) (σ.presentation i)
329	
330	instance : (isQuasicoherent R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
331	  of_iso e := by
332	    intro ⟨⟨q⟩⟩
333	    exact ⟨⟨q.ofIsIso e.hom⟩⟩
334	
335	instance {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f] (σ : M.QuasicoherentData)
336	    [σ.IsFinitePresentation] : (σ.ofIsIso f).IsFinitePresentation where
337	  isFinite_presentation i := by
338	    dsimp
339	    exact inferInstanceAs ((σ.presentation i).ofIsIso _).IsFinite
340	
341	instance : (isFinitePresentation R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
342	  of_iso e := by
343	    intro ⟨σ, hσ⟩
344	    exact ⟨σ.ofIsIso e.hom, inferInstance⟩
345	
346	end
347	
348	section bind
349	
350	variable [∀ X, (J.over X).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
351	  [∀ X, HasSheafify (J.over X) AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
352	  [∀ X, (J.over X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
353	  [∀ X Y, ((J.over X).over Y).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
354	  [∀ X Y, HasSheafify ((J.over X).over Y) AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
355	  [∀ X Y, ((J.over X).over Y).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
356	
357	#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
358	/-- Given an cover `X` and a quasicoherent data for `M` restricted onto each `Mᵢ`, we may glue them
359	into a quasicoherent data of `M` itself. -/
360	noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.bind {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
361	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
362	    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) (D : Π i, QuasicoherentData (M.over (X i))) :
363	    M.QuasicoherentData where
364	  I := Σ i, (D i).I
365	  X ij := ((D ij.1).X ij.2).left
366	  coversTop Y := J.transitive (hX Y) _ fun Z f ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ ↦
367	      J.superset_covering ((Sieve.functorPushforward_ofObjects_le _ _ _).trans
368	      (Sieve.ofObjects_mono fun i' ↦ by aesop)) ((D i).coversTop (.mk g))

module

public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.QuasiCompact
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Properties
public import Mathlib.Tactic.DepRewrite

/-!
# Ideal sheaves on schemes

We define ideal sheaves of schemes and provide various constructors for it.

## Main definition
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData`: A structure that contains the data to uniquely define
  an ideal sheaf, consisting of
  1. an ideal `I(U) ≤ Γ(X, U)` for every affine open `U`
  2. a proof that `I(D(f)) = I(U)_f` for every affine open `U` and every section `f : Γ(X, U)`.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`:
  The largest ideal sheaf contained in a family of ideals.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.equivOfIsAffine`:
  Over affine schemes, ideal sheaves are in bijection with ideals of the global sections.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.support`: The support of an ideal sheaf.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal`: The vanishing ideal of a set.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker`: The kernel of a morphism.

## Main results
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.gc`:
  `support` and `vanishingIdeal` forms a Galois connection.
* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.support_ker`: The support of a kernel of a quasi-compact morphism
  is the closure of the range.

## Implementation detail

Ideal sheaves are not yet defined in this file as actual subsheaves of `𝒪ₓ`.
Instead, for the ease of development and application,
we define the structure `IdealSheafData` containing all necessary data to uniquely define an
ideal sheaf. This should be refactored as a constructor for ideal sheaves once they are introduced
into mathlib.

-/

@[expose] public section

open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace

universe u

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/--
A structure that contains the data to uniquely define an ideal sheaf, consisting of
1. an ideal `I(U) ≤ Γ(X, U)` for every affine open `U`
2. a proof that `I(D(f)) = I(U)_f` for every affine open `U` and every section `f : Γ(X, U)`
3. a subset of `X` equal to the support.

Also see `Scheme.IdealSheafData.mkOfMemSupportIff` for a constructor with the condition on the
support being (usually) easier to prove.
-/
structure IdealSheafData (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u where
  /-- The component of an ideal sheaf at an affine open. -/
  ideal : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)
  /-- Also see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.map_ideal` -/
  map_ideal_basicOpen : ∀ (U : X.affineOpens) (f : Γ(X, U)),
    (ideal U).map (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE <| X.basicOpen_le f).op).hom =
      ideal (X.affineBasicOpen f)
  /-- The support of an ideal sheaf. Use `IdealSheafData.support` instead for most occasions. -/
  supportSet : Set X := ⋂ U, X.zeroLocus (U := U.1) (ideal U)
  supportSet_eq_iInter_zeroLocus : supportSet = ⋂ U, X.zeroLocus (U := U.1) (ideal U) := by rfl

namespace IdealSheafData

@[ext]
protected lemma ext {I J : X.IdealSheafData} (h : I.ideal = J.ideal) : I = J := by
  obtain ⟨i, _, s, hs⟩ := I
  obtain ⟨j, _, t, ht⟩ := J
  subst h
  congr
  rw [hs, ht]

section Order

instance : PartialOrder (IdealSheafData X) := PartialOrder.lift ideal fun _ _ ↦ IdealSheafData.ext

lemma le_def {I J : IdealSheafData X} : I ≤ J ↔ ∀ U, I.ideal U ≤ J.ideal U := .rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
instance : CompleteSemilatticeSup (IdealSheafData X) where
  sSup s :=
  { ideal := sSup (ideal '' s),
    map_ideal_basicOpen := by
      have : sSup (ideal '' s) = ⨆ i : s, ideal i.1 := by
        conv_lhs => rw [← Subtype.range_val (s := s), ← Set.range_comp]
        rfl
      simp only [this, iSup_apply, Ideal.map_iSup, map_ideal_basicOpen, implies_true] }
  isLUB_sSup _ := .of_image (f := ideal) le_def (isLUB_sSup _)

/-- The largest ideal sheaf contained in a family of ideals. -/
def ofIdeals (I : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)) : IdealSheafData X :=
  sSup { J : IdealSheafData X | J.ideal ≤ I }

lemma ideal_ofIdeals_le (I : ∀ U : X.affineOpens, Ideal Γ(X, U)) :
    (ofIdeals I).ideal ≤ I :=
  sSup_le (Set.forall_mem_image.mpr fun _ ↦ id)

/-- The Galois coinsertion between ideal sheaves and arbitrary families of ideals. -/
protected def gci : GaloisCoinsertion ideal (ofIdeals (X := X)) where
  choice I hI :=
  { ideal := I
    map_ideal_basicOpen U f :=
      (ideal_ofIdeals_le I).antisymm hI ▸ (ofIdeals I).map_ideal_basicOpen U f }
  gc _ _ := ⟨(le_sSup ·), (le_trans · (ideal_ofIdeals_le _))⟩
  u_l_le _ := sSup_le fun _ ↦ id
  choice_eq I hI := IdealSheafData.ext (hI.antisymm (ideal_ofIdeals_le I))

lemma strictMono_ideal : StrictMono (ideal (X := X)) := IdealSheafData.gci.strictMono_l
lemma ideal_mono : Monotone (ideal (X := X)) := strictMono_ideal.monotone
lemma ofIdeals_mono : Monotone (ofIdeals (X := X)) := IdealSheafData.gci.gc.monotone_u
lemma ofIdeals_ideal (I : IdealSheafData X) : ofIdeals I.ideal = I := IdealSheafData.gci.u_l_eq _
lemma le_ofIdeals_iff {I : IdealSheafData X} {J} : I ≤ ofIdeals J ↔ I.ideal ≤ J :=
  IdealSheafData.gci.gc.le_iff_le.symm

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
instance : OrderTop (IdealSheafData X) where
  top.ideal := ⊤
  top.map_ideal_basicOpen := by simp [Ideal.map_top]
  top.supportSet := ⊥
  top.supportSet_eq_iInter_zeroLocus := by
    ext x
    simpa using X.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ x) isOpen_univ
  le_top I U := le_top

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
instance : OrderBot (IdealSheafData X) where
  bot.ideal := ⊥

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support

These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,
§"Support and freeness predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no
annihilator ideal sheaf for sheaves of modules on a scheme, nor a schematic-support
or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.

The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/

namespace Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
(`def:modules_annihilator`).

On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).

This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)

/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _

/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
  (annihilator F).subscheme

/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
`annihilator` is. -/
noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
  (annihilator F).subschemeι

/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)

end Scheme.Modules

end AlgebraicGeometry

definable axiom-clean RIGHT NOW, with no bridge. The bridge is only needed for the *forward*
characterization lemma `(annihilator F).ideal U = Module.annihilator Γ(X,U) Γ(F,U)` (the analogue
of Mathlib's `Hom.ker_apply`), which I did NOT add.
